question.english: In your opinion, what makes a city a good one to live in?
question.chinese: 依你看，甚麼會令一座城市成為適合居住的好地方？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, what makes a city a good one to live in?
依你看，甚麼會令一座城市成為適合居住的好地方？

components.english: I think a good city to live in is one that meets people’s practical needs well, especially in terms of safety, housing, transport, and job opportunities.
components.chinese: 我認為，一座適合居住的好城市，首先必須能夠好好滿足人們的實際需要，尤其是在安全、住屋、交通，以及就業機會這幾方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think a good city to live in is one that meets people’s practical needs
well, especially in terms of safety, housing, transport, and job opportunities.
[我認為，一座適合居住的好城市，首先必須能夠好好滿足人們的實際需要，尤其是在
安全、住屋、交通，以及就業機會這幾方面。]

components.english: If a city cannot provide those basics, daily life quickly becomes an uphill battle. People need to feel safe when they go out, move around without too much hassle, and earn enough to live with some dignity. A city may look impressive on paper, but if residents are constantly worn down by high rent, long commutes, or insecurity, the shine soon wears off. In that sense, a good city is not just somewhere people survive; it is somewhere they can breathe.
components.chinese: 如果一座城市連這些基本條件都提供不到，那麼日常生活很快就會變成一場艱難的苦戰。人們需要在外出時感到安全、出行時不必過分折騰，也需要賺到足夠的收入，讓自己活得有基本尊嚴。一座城市也許表面上看起來很光鮮，但如果居民每天都被高昂租金、漫長通勤，或者不安感慢慢磨蝕，那份光彩很快就會褪去。從這個角度來看，一座好城市不應只是讓人勉強生存的地方，而應該是讓人真正可以喘息的地方。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: If a city cannot provide those basics, daily life quickly becomes
an uphill battle. People need to feel safe when they go out, move around without
too much hassle, and earn enough to live with some dignity. A city may look
impressive on paper, but if residents are constantly worn down by high rent,
long commutes, or insecurity, the shine soon wears off. In that sense, a good city
is not just somewhere people survive; it is somewhere they can breathe.
[如果一座城市連這些基本條件都提供不到，那麼日常生活很快就會變成一場艱難的
苦戰。人們需要在外出時感到安全、出行時不必過分折騰，也需要賺到足夠的收入，讓
自己活得有基本尊嚴。一座城市也許表面上看起來很光鮮，但如果居民每天都被高昂
租金、漫長通勤，或者不安感慢慢磨蝕，那份光彩很快就會褪去。從這個角度來看，一
座好城市不應只是讓人勉強生存的地方，而應該是讓人真正可以喘息的地方。]

components.english: For example, if people can get to work efficiently, find schools and hospitals within reach, and still have enough time and money left to enjoy their lives, they are much more likely to feel settled and satisfied.
components.chinese: 例如，如果人們能夠有效率地上班、在可及範圍內找到學校和醫院，而且在這之後仍然有足夠的時間和金錢去享受生活，他們自然會更容易感到安定，也更容易對這座城市感到滿意。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if people can get to work efficiently, find schools and
hospitals within reach, and still have enough time and money left to enjoy their
lives, they are much more likely to feel settled and satisfied.
[例如，如果人們能夠有效率地上班、在可及範圍內找到學校和醫院，而且在這之後仍
然有足夠的時間和金錢去享受生活，他們自然會更容易感到安定，也更容易對這座城
市感到滿意。]

components.english: So, one major thing that makes a city good to live in is a strong foundation of safety, convenience, and opportunity.
components.chinese: 所以，一座城市之所以適合居住，其中一個最重要的因素，就是它是否擁有由安全、便利和機會所構成的穩固基礎。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major thing that makes a city good to live in is a strong
foundation of safety, convenience, and opportunity.
[所以，一座城市之所以適合居住，其中一個最重要的因素，就是它是否擁有由安全、
便利和機會所構成的穩固基礎。]

components.english: That said, practical conditions alone are not enough; a truly liveable city also needs character, community, and a sense of belonging.
components.chinese: 不過，單靠實際條件其實並不足夠；一座真正宜居的城市，同時也需要有自己的氣質、社群感，以及讓人產生歸屬感的能力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, practical conditions alone are not enough; a truly liveable
city also needs character, community, and a sense of belonging.
[不過，單靠實際條件其實並不足夠；一座真正宜居的城市，同時也需要有自己的氣
質、社群感，以及讓人產生歸屬感的能力。]

components.english: To me, a city is more than bricks and mortar. People also want green spaces, cultural life, clean surroundings, and a social atmosphere that does not leave them feeling like strangers in their own neighbourhood. Even a wealthy city can feel cold at heart if it has no warmth, no rhythm, and no human touch. By contrast, a city that gives people room to connect, unwind, and enjoy life becomes more than a place to make a living; it becomes a place to have a life.
components.chinese: 在我看來，一座城市並不只是鋼筋水泥的堆砌而已。人們同時也需要綠化空間、文化生活、整潔環境，以及一種不會令人即使住在自己社區裡也像局外人的社會氣氛。即使一座城市很富裕，如果它沒有溫度、沒有節奏，也缺乏人情味，它仍然可能讓人感到冷冰冰。相反，一座能夠給人空間去建立連結、放鬆自己，以及享受生活的城市，就不再只是一個謀生的地方，而會變成一個真正可以生活的地方。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To me, a city is more than bricks and mortar. People also want
green spaces, cultural life, clean surroundings, and a social atmosphere that does
not leave them feeling like strangers in their own neighbourhood. Even a
wealthy city can feel cold at heart if it has no warmth, no rhythm, and no
human touch. By contrast, a city that gives people room to connect, unwind,
and enjoy life becomes more than a place to make a living; it becomes a place to
have a life.
[在我看來，一座城市並不只是鋼筋水泥的堆砌而已。人們同時也需要綠化空間、文化
生活、整潔環境，以及一種不會令人即使住在自己社區裡也像局外人的社會氣氛。即
使一座城市很富裕，如果它沒有溫度、沒有節奏，也缺乏人情味，它仍然可能讓人感到
冷冰冰。相反，一座能夠給人空間去建立連結、放鬆自己，以及享受生活的城市，就不
再只是一個謀生的地方，而會變成一個真正可以生活的地方。]

components.english: For instance, when a city has parks, libraries, museums, waterfronts, and community events where people can meet, relax, and feel part of something larger, residents usually develop a much stronger attachment to it.
components.chinese: 例如，當一座城市擁有公園、圖書館、博物館、海濱空間，以及各種讓人可以相聚、放鬆，並感覺自己屬於某個更大共同體的社區活動時，居民通常都會對那座城市產生更深厚的感情。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when a city has parks, libraries, museums,
waterfronts, and community events where people can meet, relax, and feel part
of something larger, residents usually develop a much stronger attachment to it.
[例如，當一座城市擁有公園、圖書館、博物館、海濱空間，以及各種讓人可以相聚、放
鬆，並感覺自己屬於某個更大共同體的社區活動時，居民通常都會對那座城市產生更
深厚的感情。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a good city is one that combines practical liveability with human warmth. It should offer not only jobs and infrastructure, but also balance, dignity, and a real sense of home.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說一座好城市，應該把實際上的宜居條件和人情溫度結合起來。它不但要提供工作和基礎建設，也要給人平衡感、尊嚴，以及一種真正稱得上「家」的感覺。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a good city is one that combines practical
liveability with human warmth. It should offer not only jobs and infrastructure,
but also balance, dignity, and a real sense of home.
[總結來說，我會說一座好城市，應該把實際上的宜居條件和人情溫度結合起來。它不
但要提供工作和基礎建設，也要給人平衡感、尊嚴，以及一種真正稱得上「家」的感

question.english: What are the advantages of living in a city?
question.chinese: 居住在城市裡有甚麼好處？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the advantages of living in a city?
居住在城市裡有甚麼好處？

components.english: I think one of the biggest advantages of living in a city is convenience, because so many essential services and facilities are right on your doorstep.
components.chinese: 我認為，住在城市其中一個最大的好處就是方便，因為很多基本服務和設施都近在咫尺。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the biggest advantages of living in a city is convenience,
because so many essential services and facilities are right on your doorstep.
[我認為，住在城市其中一個最大的好處就是方便，因為很多基本服務和設施都近在
咫尺。]

components.english: In a city, people usually have easier access to public transport, hospitals, schools, supermarkets, restaurants, and entertainment venues. That makes daily life smoother and far less time-consuming. Instead of bending over backwards just to get from one place to another, people can often get things done quickly and efficiently. In that sense, city life puts time back in your hands, and that can make a real difference, especially for people with busy schedules.
components.chinese: 在城市裡，人們通常更容易接觸到公共交通、醫院、學校、超市、餐廳，以及各種娛樂場所。這會令日常生活更加順暢，也不用花那麼多時間。人們不需要為了去不同地方而費盡周章，很多事情都可以更快、更有效率地完成。從這個角度來看，城市生活某程度上是把時間還給人，而這對生活忙碌的人來說尤其重要。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In a city, people usually have easier access to public transport,
hospitals, schools, supermarkets, restaurants, and entertainment venues. That
makes daily life smoother and far less time-consuming. Instead of bending over
backwards just to get from one place to another, people can often get things
done quickly and efficiently. In that sense, city life puts time back in your hands,
and that can make a real difference, especially for people with busy schedules.
[在城市裡，人們通常更容易接觸到公共交通、醫院、學校、超市、餐廳，以及各種娛樂
場所。這會令日常生活更加順暢，也不用花那麼多時間。人們不需要為了去不同地方
而費盡周章，很多事情都可以更快、更有效率地完成。從這個角度來看，城市生活某程
度上是把時間還給人，而這對生活忙碌的人來說尤其重要。]

components.english: For example, if someone lives in a large city, they may be able to commute by train, pick up groceries on the way home, and meet friends for dinner without travelling long distances. By contrast, in a less developed area, even simple errands can eat up half the day.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人住在大城市，他可能可以乘火車上下班、回家途中順便買菜，然後晚上再和朋友吃飯，而不需要花很長時間來回奔波。相比之下，在一些發展較少的地方，就連處理一些簡單瑣事，也可能耗掉半天時間。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone lives in a large city, they may be able to
commute by train, pick up groceries on the way home, and meet friends for
dinner without travelling long distances. By contrast, in a less developed area,
even simple errands can eat up half the day.
[例如，如果一個人住在大城市，他可能可以乘火車上下班、回家途中順便買菜，然後
晚上再和朋友吃飯，而不需要花很長時間來回奔波。相比之下，在一些發展較少的地
方，就連處理一些簡單瑣事，也可能耗掉半天時間。]

components.english: So, I’d say convenience is a major advantage of city life, because it allows people to live more efficiently and with less friction in their daily routine.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說方便是城市生活的一大優勢，因為它能讓人以更有效率、也更少阻力的方式去過日常生活。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I’d say convenience is a major advantage of city life, because it
allows people to live more efficiently and with less friction in their daily routine.
[所以，我會說方便是城市生活的一大優勢，因為它能讓人以更有效率、也更少阻力的
方式去過日常生活。]

components.english: On top of that, living in a city often opens the door to better opportunities, both professionally and socially.
components.chinese: 除此之外，住在城市通常也能帶來更多機會，無論是在職業發展還是社交方面都是如此。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, living in a city often opens the door to better
opportunities, both professionally and socially.
[除此之外，住在城市通常也能帶來更多機會，無論是在職業發展還是社交方面都是
如此。]

components.english: Cities tend to be the beating heart of education, business, and culture. There are usually more jobs, more networking opportunities, and a wider range of courses, events, and experiences. As a result, people are more likely to broaden their horizons and grow as individuals. A city can be a bit like a crossroads: different industries, ideas, and people all meet there, and that creates an environment in which ambition has room to breathe.
components.chinese: 城市往往是一個地方在教育、商業和文化上的核心地帶。那裡通常有更多工作機會、更多建立人脈的機會，以及更廣泛的課程、活動和體驗。因此，人們更有可能開闊眼界，並在個人成長上走得更遠。城市有點像一個交匯點：不同的行業、想法和人都在那裡相遇，而這就形成了一個能讓理想與野心有空間發展的環境。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Cities tend to be the beating heart of education, business, and
culture. There are usually more jobs, more networking opportunities, and a
wider range of courses, events, and experiences. As a result, people are more
likely to broaden their horizons and grow as individuals. A city can be a bit like
a crossroads: different industries, ideas, and people all meet there, and that
creates an environment in which ambition has room to breathe.
[城市往往是一個地方在教育、商業和文化上的核心地帶。那裡通常有更多工作機會、
更多建立人脈的機會，以及更廣泛的課程、活動和體驗。因此，人們更有可能開闊眼界
，並在個人成長上走得更遠。城市有點像一個交匯點：不同的行業、想法和人都在那裡
相遇，而這就形成了一個能讓理想與野心有空間發展的環境。]

components.english: For instance, a young graduate living in a city may find it much easier to attend interviews, take evening classes, go to professional events, and meet people from different backgrounds. That kind of exposure can work wonders, because it helps people build skills, confidence, and perspective all at once.
components.chinese: 例如，一位住在城市的年輕畢業生，通常會更容易參加面試、報讀晚間課程、出席專業活動，並接觸來自不同背景的人。這種接觸面往往很有幫助，因為它能同時提升一個人的能力、自信，以及看事情的角度。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a young graduate living in a city may find it much
easier to attend interviews, take evening classes, go to professional events, and
meet people from different backgrounds. That kind of exposure can work
wonders, because it helps people build skills, confidence, and perspective all at
once.
[例如，一位住在城市的年輕畢業生，通常會更容易參加面試、報讀晚間課程、出席專
業活動，並接觸來自不同背景的人。這種接觸面往往很有幫助，因為它能同時提升一
個人的能力、自信，以及看事情的角度。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the main advantages of living in a city are convenience and opportunity. Granted, city life can sometimes feel fast-paced or even overwhelming, but it undeniably gives people better access to resources, connections, and the kind of experiences that can help them move forward in life.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說住在城市的主要好處在於它既方便，又充滿機會。雖然城市生活有時節奏很快，甚至會讓人喘不過氣，但它確實能讓人更容易接觸資源、建立連結，並追求自己想要的生活。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the main advantages of living in a city are
convenience and opportunity. Granted, city life can sometimes feel fast-paced or
even overwhelming, but it undeniably gives people better access to resources,
connections, and the kind of experiences that can help them move forward in
life.
[總結來說，我會說住在城市的主要好處在於它既方便，又充滿機會。雖然城市生活有
時節奏很快，甚至會讓人喘不過氣，但它確實能讓人更容易接觸資源、建立連結，並追
求自己想要的生活。]

question.english: In your opinion, what are the negative aspects of crowded cities?
question.chinese: 依你看，擠迫城市有哪些負面之處？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, what are the negative aspects of crowded cities?
依你看，擠迫城市有哪些負面之處？

components.english: I think one of the biggest negative aspects of crowded cities is that they put constant pressure on daily life, especially in terms of housing, transport, and the general cost of living.
components.chinese: 我認為，擠迫城市其中一個最大的負面之處，就是它會對日常生活造成持續壓力，特別是在住屋、交通，以及整體生活成本方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the biggest negative aspects of crowded cities is that they
put constant pressure on daily life, especially in terms of housing, transport, and
the general cost of living.
[我認為，擠迫城市其中一個最大的負面之處，就是它會對日常生活造成持續壓力，特
別是在住屋、交通，以及整體生活成本方面。]

components.english: When too many people are squeezed into too little space, even ordinary routines can start to feel like a grind. Public transport becomes packed, roads become congested, and housing often becomes painfully expensive. On top of that, noise, pollution, and a lack of personal space can wear people down bit by bit. In other words, a crowded city may offer plenty of opportunity, but it can also make everyday life feel like an uphill battle.
components.chinese: 當太多人被擠進太小的空間時，連最平常的日常生活都可能慢慢變成一種消耗。公共交通會變得非常擠迫，道路會塞車，而住屋往往也會貴得令人吃不消。除此之外，噪音、污染，以及缺乏私人空間，也會一點一滴地把人磨得疲憊不堪。換句話說，擠迫的城市也許充滿機會，但同時也可能令日常生活變成一場艱難的苦戰。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When too many people are squeezed into too little space, even
ordinary routines can start to feel like a grind. Public transport becomes packed,
roads become congested, and housing often becomes painfully expensive. On top
of that, noise, pollution, and a lack of personal space can wear people down bit
by bit. In other words, a crowded city may offer plenty of opportunity, but it
can also make everyday life feel like an uphill battle.
[當太多人被擠進太小的空間時，連最平常的日常生活都可能慢慢變成一種消耗。公
共交通會變得非常擠迫，道路會塞車，而住屋往往也會貴得令人吃不消。除此之外，噪
音、污染，以及缺乏私人空間，也會一點一滴地把人磨得疲憊不堪。換句話說，擠迫的
城市也許充滿機會，但同時也可能令日常生活變成一場艱難的苦戰。]

components.english: For example, many people in large cities have to spend a long time commuting on overcrowded trains, only to go home to a tiny flat that costs a fortune. If that happens day in, day out, it is no surprise that people start to feel stressed and drained.
components.chinese: 例如，在大城市裡，很多人每天都要花很長時間擠在爆滿的列車上通勤，回到家後卻只是住進一個面積很小、但租金或樓價高得驚人的單位。如果這樣的生活日復一日地持續下去，人們會開始感到壓力沉重、身心俱疲，其實一點也不令人意外。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, many people in large cities have to spend a long time
commuting on overcrowded trains, only to go home to a tiny flat that costs a
fortune. If that happens day in, day out, it is no surprise that people start to feel
stressed and drained.
[例如，在大城市裡，很多人每天都要花很長時間擠在爆滿的列車上通勤，回到家後卻
只是住進一個面積很小、但租金或樓價高得驚人的單位。如果這樣的生活日復一日地
持續下去，人們會開始感到壓力沉重、身心俱疲，其實一點也不令人意外。]

components.english: So, one clear downside of crowded cities is that they can make life more expensive, more tiring, and more stressful than it needs to be.
components.chinese: 所以，擠迫城市其中一個很明顯的壞處，就是它會令生活變得比本來更昂貴、更疲累，也更有壓力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear downside of crowded cities is that they can make life
more expensive, more tiring, and more stressful than it needs to be.
[所以，擠迫城市其中一個很明顯的壞處，就是它會令生活變得比本來更昂貴、更疲累
，也更有壓力。]

components.english: Another major problem is the emotional and social impact, because crowded cities can be strangely isolating even when people are surrounded by others.
components.chinese: 另一個主要問題，就是它在情感和社交層面上的影響，因為擠迫城市即使四周都是人，卻仍然可以令人感到非常孤立。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another major problem is the emotional and social impact, because
crowded cities can be strangely isolating even when people are surrounded by
others.
[另一個主要問題，就是它在情感和社交層面上的影響，因為擠迫城市即使四周都是
人，卻仍然可以令人感到非常孤立。]

components.english: To be honest, crowded cities can sometimes feel like a pressure cooker. People are always rushing, always queuing, always dealing with noise and pressure, so they may become impatient, emotionally tired, or less connected to the people around them. There is also a strange paradox there: a person can be in the middle of a sea of people and still feel completely alone. When life moves at breakneck speed, it becomes harder to build strong community ties or simply slow down long enough to enjoy life.
components.chinese: 老實說，擠迫城市有時真的會像一個高壓鍋。人們總是在趕路、總是在排隊、總是在應付噪音和壓力，所以他們很容易變得不耐煩、情緒疲憊，或者和身邊的人失去連結。當中其實有一種很奇怪的矛盾：一個人明明身處在人海中央，卻仍然可能感到非常孤單。當生活節奏快得驚人時，人們就更難建立穩固的社區關係，甚至連好好慢下來享受生活都變得困難。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, crowded cities can sometimes feel like a pressure
cooker. People are always rushing, always queuing, always dealing with noise and
pressure, so they may become impatient, emotionally tired, or less connected to
the people around them. There is also a strange paradox there: a person can be
in the middle of a sea of people and still feel completely alone. When life moves
at breakneck speed, it becomes harder to build strong community ties or simply
slow down long enough to enjoy life.
[老實說，擠迫城市有時真的會像一個高壓鍋。人們總是在趕路、總是在排隊、總是在
應付噪音和壓力，所以他們很容易變得不耐煩、情緒疲憊，或者和身邊的人失去連結。
當中其實有一種很奇怪的矛盾：一個人明明身處在人海中央，卻仍然可能感到非常孤
單。當生活節奏快得驚人時，人們就更難建立穩固的社區關係，甚至連好好慢下來享
受生活都變得困難。]

components.english: For instance, someone may spend the whole day surrounded by crowds at work, on the street, and on public transport, yet still feel lonely because all those interactions are rushed and superficial. In that kind of environment, burnout can creep in before people even realise it.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能整天在工作場所、街上，以及公共交通裡都被人群包圍，但仍然感到孤單，因為那些接觸往往都很匆忙，也很表面。在這樣的環境裡，倦怠感往往會在不知不覺間慢慢滲進生活。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may spend the whole day surrounded by
crowds at work, on the street, and on public transport, yet still feel lonely
because all those interactions are rushed and superficial. In that kind of
environment, burnout can creep in before people even realise it.
[例如，一個人可能整天在工作場所、街上，以及公共交通裡都被人群包圍，但仍然感
到孤單，因為那些接觸往往都很匆忙，也很表面。在這樣的環境裡，倦怠感往往會在不
知不覺間慢慢滲進生活。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the negative aspects of crowded cities are not just physical, but also emotional. They may offer energy and excitement, but when overcrowding goes too far, the buzz can quickly turn into a burden.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說擠迫城市的負面之處不只是身體層面上的，也是情感層面上的。它們也許帶來活力和熱鬧，但當擠迫去得太遠時，那份熱鬧很快就會變成一種負擔。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the negative aspects of crowded cities are not
just physical, but also emotional. They may offer energy and excitement, but
when overcrowding goes too far, the buzz can quickly turn into a burden.
[總結來說，我會說擠迫城市的負面之處不只是身體層面上的，也是情感層面上的。它
們也許帶來活力和熱鬧，但當擠迫去得太遠時，那份熱鬧很快就會變成一種負擔。]

question.english: How can governments improve living standards in crowded cities?
question.chinese: 在擠迫的城市裡，政府可以怎樣改善生活質素？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can governments improve living standards in crowded cities?
在擠迫的城市裡，政府可以怎樣改善生活質素？

components.english: I think the most effective way is to invest in better urban planning and stronger public services, because crowded cities become much harder to live in when basic systems are stretched to the limit.
components.chinese: 我認為，最有效的方法是投放資源於更好的城市規劃和更完善的公共服務，因為當一座擠迫的城市連基本系統都被拉扯到極限時，生活自然會變得困難得多。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the most effective way is to invest in better urban planning and
stronger public services, because crowded cities become much harder to live in
when basic systems are stretched to the limit.
[我認為，最有效的方法是投放資源於更好的城市規劃和更完善的公共服務，因為當
一座擠迫的城市連基本系統都被拉扯到極限時，生活自然會變得困難得多。]

components.english: In many crowded cities, the real problem is not just the number of people; it is the gap between population growth and infrastructure. If housing is unaffordable, public transport is unreliable, and medical or educational services are overloaded, daily life starts to feel like a grind. Governments therefore need to build more affordable housing, improve transport networks, and make sure essential services can keep pace with demand. Otherwise, people end up paying more, travelling longer, and living under constant pressure.
components.chinese: 在很多擠迫城市裡，真正的問題不只是人口多，而是人口增長和基建發展之間出現落差。如果住屋難以負擔、公共交通不可靠，而醫療或教育服務又不堪重負，日常生活就會變成一場消耗戰。所以，政府需要興建更多可負擔房屋、改善交通網絡，並確保基本公共服務能跟得上需求。否則，人們就會變成花更多錢、花更長時間通勤，還要長期承受壓力。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In many crowded cities, the real problem is not just the number
of people; it is the gap between population growth and infrastructure. If
housing is unaffordable, public transport is unreliable, and medical or
educational services are overloaded, daily life starts to feel like a grind.
Governments therefore need to build more affordable housing, improve
transport networks, and make sure essential services can keep pace with
demand. Otherwise, people end up paying more, travelling longer, and living
under constant pressure.
[在很多擠迫城市裡，真正的問題不只是人口多，而是人口增長和基建發展之間出現
落差。如果住屋難以負擔、公共交通不可靠，而醫療或教育服務又不堪重負，日常生活
就會變成一場消耗戰。所以，政府需要興建更多可負擔房屋、改善交通網絡，並確保基
本公共服務能跟得上需求。否則，人們就會變成花更多錢、花更長時間通勤，還要長期
承受壓力。]

components.english: For example, if a government builds affordable flats near transport hubs, expands train lines, and opens more local clinics and schools, people can save both time and energy. Instead of spending hours commuting or waiting for services, they can have a more manageable and less exhausting daily routine.
components.chinese: 例如，如果政府在交通樞紐附近興建可負擔住宅、擴展鐵路網絡，並增設社區診所和學校，人們就可以同時節省時間和精力。這樣一來，他們不用再花幾個小時通勤，或者長時間輪候服務，日常生活也會變得較容易應付，沒那麼疲憊。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a government builds affordable flats near transport
hubs, expands train lines, and opens more local clinics and schools, people can
save both time and energy. Instead of spending hours commuting or waiting for
services, they can have a more manageable and less exhausting daily routine.
[例如，如果政府在交通樞紐附近興建可負擔住宅、擴展鐵路網絡，並增設社區診所和
學校，人們就可以同時節省時間和精力。這樣一來，他們不用再花幾個小時通勤，或者
長時間輪候服務，日常生活也會變得較容易應付，沒那麼疲憊。]

components.english: So, to me, improving living standards in crowded cities starts with getting the basics right, because when housing, transport, and public services run smoothly, the whole city breathes more easily.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，要改善擠迫城市的生活質素，首先就是要把最基本的事情做好，因為當住屋、交通和公共服務運作順暢時，整座城市都會像終於能順一口氣那樣，舒服得多。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, improving living standards in crowded cities starts
with getting the basics right, because when housing, transport, and public
services run smoothly, the whole city breathes more easily.
[所以，在我看來，要改善擠迫城市的生活質素，首先就是要把最基本的事情做好，因
為當住屋、交通和公共服務運作順暢時，整座城市都會像終於能順一口氣那樣，舒服
得多。]

components.english: On top of that, governments should make crowded cities more humane by creating cleaner, greener, and safer living environments.
components.chinese: 除此之外，政府也應該令擠迫的城市變得更有人味，方法就是打造更清潔、更綠化、也更安全的生活環境。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, governments should make crowded cities more
humane by creating cleaner, greener, and safer living environments.
[除此之外，政府也應該令擠迫的城市變得更有人味，方法就是打造更清潔、更綠化、
也更安全的生活環境。]

components.english: A city should not feel like a concrete jungle where people simply squeeze through life. Even in a densely populated place, governments can improve well-being by adding parks, controlling pollution, protecting pedestrian areas, and providing community facilities. Living standards are not measured by income alone; they also depend on whether people have space to rest, clean air to breathe, and a neighbourhood that feels safe and livable. In other words, a good city should do more than function; it should allow people to live with dignity.
components.chinese: 一座城市不應該像鋼筋水泥森林一樣，讓人只能在夾縫中勉強生活。即使在高密度地區，政府仍然可以透過增加公園、控制污染、保障行人空間，以及提供社區設施來改善市民福祉。生活質素並不只是看收入高低，還取決於人們是否有空間休息、有乾淨空氣可以呼吸，以及是否身處一個安全而宜居的社區。換句話說，一個好的城市不應只是能運作而已；它更應該讓人活得有尊嚴。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A city should not feel like a concrete jungle where people
simply squeeze through life. Even in a densely populated place, governments can
improve well-being by adding parks, controlling pollution, protecting
pedestrian areas, and providing community facilities. Living standards are not
measured by income alone; they also depend on whether people have space to
rest, clean air to breathe, and a neighbourhood that feels safe and livable. In
other words, a good city should do more than function; it should allow people
to live with dignity.
[一座城市不應該像鋼筋水泥森林一樣，讓人只能在夾縫中勉強生活。即使在高密度
地區，政府仍然可以透過增加公園、控制污染、保障行人空間，以及提供社區設施來改
善市民福祉。生活質素並不只是看收入高低，還取決於人們是否有空間休息、有乾淨
空氣可以呼吸，以及是否身處一個安全而宜居的社區。換句話說，一個好的城市不應
只是能運作而已；它更應該讓人活得有尊嚴。]

components.english: For instance, if the government turns unused land into small parks, adds more cycling paths, improves street lighting, and sets stricter air-quality standards, residents may feel the difference almost immediately. Families would have somewhere to relax, children could play more safely, and older people might find the neighbourhood far more pleasant to live in.
components.chinese: 例如，如果政府把閒置土地改造成小型公園、增設單車徑、改善街道照明，並訂立更嚴格的空氣質素標準，居民往往很快就能感受到分別。家庭會有地方放鬆，小朋友可以更安全地玩耍，而長者也可能會覺得整個社區明顯更適合居住。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if the government turns unused land into small
parks, adds more cycling paths, improves street lighting, and sets stricter
air-quality standards, residents may feel the difference almost immediately.
Families would have somewhere to relax, children could play more safely, and
older people might find the neighbourhood far more pleasant to live in.
[例如，如果政府把閒置土地改造成小型公園、增設單車徑、改善街道照明，並訂立更
嚴格的空氣質素標準，居民往往很快就能感受到分別。家庭會有地方放鬆，小朋友可
以更安全地玩耍，而長者也可能會覺得整個社區明顯更適合居住。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say governments can improve living standards in crowded cities by tackling both efficiency and quality of life. They need to make cities work better, but they also need to make them feel better.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說政府要改善擠迫城市的生活質素，就必須同時處理效率和生活感受這兩方面。他們既要令城市運作得更好，也要令城市住起來更舒服。真正宜居的城市，不只是能容納很多人，而是能讓很多人都好好生活。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say governments can improve living standards in crowded
cities by tackling both efficiency and quality of life. They need to make cities work
better, but they also need to make them feel better.
[總結來說，我會說政府要改善擠迫城市的生活質素，就必須同時處理效率和生活感受這兩
方面。他們既要令城市運作得更好，也要令城市住起來更舒服。真正宜居的城市，不只是能
容納很多人，而是能讓很多人都好好生活。]

question.english: What can people do to improve the air quality in the city?
question.chinese: 在城市裡，人們可以做些甚麼去改善空氣質素？

source_lines.text: Topic: What can people do to improve the air quality in the city?
在城市裡，人們可以做些甚麼去改善空氣質素？

components.english: I think one of the most effective things people can do is reduce their reliance on private cars and use cleaner forms of transport instead.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們其中一個最有效可以做的事情，就是減少依賴私家車，改用較潔淨的交通方式。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the most effective things people can do is reduce their
reliance on private cars and use cleaner forms of transport instead.
[我認為，人們其中一個最有效可以做的事情，就是減少依賴私家車，改用較潔淨的交
通方式。]

components.english: The reason is that traffic is often a major source of urban air pollution, especially in busy cities where the roads are constantly clogged up. If more people take public transport, walk, cycle, or carpool, fewer vehicles will be pumping exhaust fumes into the air. In other words, cleaner commuting can help take some of the smoke out of the city’s lungs.
components.chinese: 原因是交通往往是城市空氣污染的主要來源之一，尤其是在道路長期擠塞的繁忙城市裡更是如此。如果更多人乘搭公共交通、步行、踩單車，或者共乘車輛，路上排放廢氣的汽車就會減少。換句話說，更潔淨的出行方式可以幫助城市的「肺」少吸一點煙霧。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that traffic is often a major source of urban air
pollution, especially in busy cities where the roads are constantly clogged up. If
more people take public transport, walk, cycle, or carpool, fewer vehicles will be
pumping exhaust fumes into the air. In other words, cleaner commuting can
help take some of the smoke out of the city’s lungs.
[原因是交通往往是城市空氣污染的主要來源之一，尤其是在道路長期擠塞的繁忙城
市裡更是如此。如果更多人乘搭公共交通、步行、踩單車，或者共乘車輛，路上排放廢
氣的汽車就會減少。換句話說，更潔淨的出行方式可以幫助城市的「肺」少吸一點煙

components.english: For example, if office workers choose the train or bus instead of driving alone every day, the overall number of cars on the road can fall quite noticeably. Even small changes, such as walking to nearby shops rather than driving there, can add up over time.
components.chinese: 例如，如果上班族每天選擇搭地鐵或巴士，而不是自己一個人開車上班，路上的車輛總數就可能明顯下降。即使只是一些小改變，例如去附近的商店時改為步行而不是開車，長久下來也會累積出效果。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if office workers choose the train or bus instead of
driving alone every day, the overall number of cars on the road can fall quite
noticeably. Even small changes, such as walking to nearby shops rather than
driving there, can add up over time.
[例如，如果上班族每天選擇搭地鐵或巴士，而不是自己一個人開車上班，路上的車輛
總數就可能明顯下降。即使只是一些小改變，例如去附近的商店時改為步行而不是開
車，長久下來也會累積出效果。]

components.english: So, from my point of view, changing the way people travel is one of the clearest and most practical ways to improve air quality in a city.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，改變人們的出行方式，是改善城市空氣質素最直接、也最實際的方法之一。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from my point of view, changing the way people travel is one
of the clearest and most practical ways to improve air quality in a city.
[所以，在我看來，改變人們的出行方式，是改善城市空氣質素最直接、也最實際的方
法之一。]

components.english: On top of that, people can help by living in a more environmentally responsible way in their daily lives.
components.chinese: 除此之外，人們也可以透過在日常生活中活得更有環保意識，去幫助改善空氣質素。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, people can help by living in a more environmentally
responsible way in their daily lives.
[除此之外，人們也可以透過在日常生活中活得更有環保意識，去幫助改善空氣質

components.english: Air pollution does not only come from traffic; it is also linked to the way people consume energy and handle waste. If people save electricity, use energy-efficient appliances, avoid unnecessary packaging, and support greener businesses, they can help reduce pollution further up the chain. It may not look dramatic at first glance, but when thousands or even millions of people make greener choices, the effect can snowball.
components.chinese: 空氣污染不只是來自交通，它也和人們如何使用能源，以及怎樣處理廢物有關。如果人們節省用電、使用節能電器、避免不必要的包裝，並支持較環保的商店或企業，他們其實可以從源頭鏈的更上游減少污染。這些改變乍看之下未必很震撼，但當成千上萬，甚至數以百萬計的人都作出較環保的選擇時，效果就會像雪球一樣愈滾愈大。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Air pollution does not only come from traffic; it is also linked
to the way people consume energy and handle waste. If people save electricity,
use energy-efficient appliances, avoid unnecessary packaging, and support
greener businesses, they can help reduce pollution further up the chain. It may
not look dramatic at first glance, but when thousands or even millions of people
make greener choices, the effect can snowball.
[空氣污染不只是來自交通，它也和人們如何使用能源，以及怎樣處理廢物有關。如果
人們節省用電、使用節能電器、避免不必要的包裝，並支持較環保的商店或企業，他們
其實可以從源頭鏈的更上游減少污染。這些改變乍看之下未必很震撼，但當成千上萬
，甚至數以百萬計的人都作出較環保的選擇時，效果就會像雪球一樣愈滾愈大。]

components.english: For instance, families can switch off lights and air conditioning when they are not needed, bring their own bags and bottles, and support local markets or brands that care about sustainability. People can also join tree-planting activities or neighbourhood clean-up events, because cleaner and greener surroundings usually go hand in hand with better air.
components.chinese: 例如，家庭可以在不需要時關掉電燈和冷氣、自備購物袋和水樽，並支持重視可持續發展的本地市場或品牌。人們也可以參與植樹活動或社區清潔行動，因為更乾淨、更綠化的環境，通常都和更好的空氣質素息息相關。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, families can switch off lights and air conditioning
when they are not needed, bring their own bags and bottles, and support local
markets or brands that care about sustainability. People can also join
tree-planting activities or neighbourhood clean-up events, because cleaner and
greener surroundings usually go hand in hand with better air.
[例如，家庭可以在不需要時關掉電燈和冷氣、自備購物袋和水樽，並支持重視可持續
發展的本地市場或品牌。人們也可以參與植樹活動或社區清潔行動，因為更乾淨、更
綠化的環境，通常都和更好的空氣質素息息相關。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say improving air quality in a city is not just the government’s responsibility; ordinary people can also play their part. Whether it is by travelling more wisely or living more greenly, small actions can still clear the air bit by bit.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說改善城市空氣質素並不只是政府的責任，市民自己也可以出一分力。無論是透過更明智地出行，還是以更環保的方式生活，細小的行動都能一點一滴地讓空氣變得更清新。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say improving air quality in a city is not just the
government’s responsibility; ordinary people can also play their part. Whether it
is by travelling more wisely or living more greenly, small actions can still clear
the air bit by bit.
[總結來說，我會說改善城市空氣質素並不只是政府的責任，市民自己也可以出一分
力。無論是透過更明智地出行，還是以更環保的方式生活，細小的行動都能一點一滴
地讓空氣變得更清新。]

question.english: What are the advantages and disadvantages of living in tall buildings?
question.chinese: 居住在高樓大廈有哪些優點和缺點？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the advantages and disadvantages of living in tall buildings?
居住在高樓大廈有哪些優點和缺點？

components.english: One major advantage of living in tall buildings is that they make city life far more practical, especially in places where land is limited.
components.chinese: 居住在高樓大廈的一個主要優點，是它能令城市生活變得更實際，尤其是在土地有限的地方。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: One major advantage of living in tall buildings is that they make city
life far more practical, especially in places where land is limited.
[居住在高樓大廈的一個主要優點，是它能令城市生活變得更實際，尤其是在土地有
限的地方。]

components.english: Tall buildings allow cities to house a large number of people without spreading endlessly outward, so they are often the most efficient use of space. On top of that, many high-rise buildings are located in convenient areas close to transport, shops, schools, and workplaces. In that sense, a tall residential building can function almost like a vertical village, with everything people need within easy reach. For busy city dwellers, that kind of convenience is worth its weight in gold.
components.chinese: 高樓大廈能讓城市在不斷向外擴張的情況下，容納大量人口，所以它們通常是最有效率地運用空間的方法。除此之外，很多高層住宅都位於交通、商店、學校和工作地點都很方便的區域。從這個角度來看，一座高層住宅幾乎就像一個垂直村落，讓人們所需的東西都近在咫尺。對生活忙碌的城市居民來說，這種便利其實非常寶貴。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Tall buildings allow cities to house a large number of people
without spreading endlessly outward, so they are often the most efficient use of
space. On top of that, many high-rise buildings are located in convenient areas
close to transport, shops, schools, and workplaces. In that sense, a tall residential
building can function almost like a vertical village, with everything people need
within easy reach. For busy city dwellers, that kind of convenience is worth its
weight in gold.
[高樓大廈能讓城市在不斷向外擴張的情況下，容納大量人口，所以它們通常是最有
效率地運用空間的方法。除此之外，很多高層住宅都位於交通、商店、學校和工作地點
都很方便的區域。從這個角度來看，一座高層住宅幾乎就像一個垂直村落，讓人們所
需的東西都近在咫尺。對生活忙碌的城市居民來說，這種便利其實非常寶貴。]

components.english: For example, someone living in a high-rise apartment may be able to walk to the subway, buy groceries downstairs, and get to work much faster than a person living far away in a low-density area. In many cities, that can make daily life smoother and more manageable.
components.chinese: 例如，一個住在高層住宅裡的人，可能可以步行到地鐵站、在樓下買日用品，並且比住在低密度地區、離市中心很遠的人更快到達工作地點。在很多城市裡，這確實能令日常生活更順暢，也更容易應付。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone living in a high-rise apartment may be able to
walk to the subway, buy groceries downstairs, and get to work much faster than
a person living far away in a low-density area. In many cities, that can make
daily life smoother and more manageable.
[例如，一個住在高層住宅裡的人，可能可以步行到地鐵站、在樓下買日用品，並且比
住在低密度地區、離市中心很遠的人更快到達工作地點。在很多城市裡，這確實能令
日常生活更順暢，也更容易應付。]

components.english: So, one clear advantage of tall buildings is that they support efficient, convenient, and highly connected urban living.
components.chinese: 所以，高樓大廈其中一個很明顯的優點，就是它能支撐一種高效率、方便，而且彼此連結緊密的城市生活方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear advantage of tall buildings is that they support
efficient, convenient, and highly connected urban living.
[所以，高樓大廈其中一個很明顯的優點，就是它能支撐一種高效率、方便，而且彼此
連結緊密的城市生活方式。]

components.english: That said, one major disadvantage is that living in tall buildings can sometimes feel cramped, impersonal, and disconnected from nature.
components.chinese: 不過，其中一個主要缺點是，居住在高樓大廈裡有時會令人感到擠迫、缺乏人情味，而且與自然脫節。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, one major disadvantage is that living in tall buildings can
sometimes feel cramped, impersonal, and disconnected from nature.
[不過，其中一個主要缺點是，居住在高樓大廈裡有時會令人感到擠迫、缺乏人情味，
而且與自然脫節。]

components.english: When people live high above the ground in small flats, life can start to feel boxed in. There may be less privacy than people expect, less outdoor space, and a greater sense of being surrounded by concrete rather than by breathing room. On top of that, daily life in tall buildings often depends heavily on lifts, management systems, and shared facilities, so when something goes wrong, the inconvenience can snowball quite quickly. In other words, high-rise living may be efficient, but it is not always easy on the mind or the nerves.
components.chinese: 當人們住在高高的樓層、面積又不大的單位裡時，生活有時會開始讓人感到被困住。私人空間可能比想像中少，戶外空間也較不足，整體上更容易讓人有一種被水泥森林包圍、缺乏喘息空間的感覺。除此之外，高樓生活往往非常依賴升降機、管理系統，以及共用設施，所以一旦哪個環節出問題，不便之處很快就可能越滾越大。換句話說，高層住宅生活也許有效率，但未必總是對人的心情和神經最友善。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: When people live high above the ground in small flats, life can
start to feel boxed in. There may be less privacy than people expect, less outdoor
space, and a greater sense of being surrounded by concrete rather than by
breathing room. On top of that, daily life in tall buildings often depends heavily
on lifts, management systems, and shared facilities, so when something goes
wrong, the inconvenience can snowball quite quickly. In other words, high-rise
living may be efficient, but it is not always easy on the mind or the nerves.
[當人們住在高高的樓層、面積又不大的單位裡時，生活有時會開始讓人感到被困住。
私人空間可能比想像中少，戶外空間也較不足，整體上更容易讓人有一種被水泥森林
包圍、缺乏喘息空間的感覺。除此之外，高樓生活往往非常依賴升降機、管理系統，以
及共用設施，所以一旦哪個環節出問題，不便之處很快就可能越滾越大。換句話說，高
層住宅生活也許有效率，但未必總是對人的心情和神經最友善。]

components.english: For instance, if the lifts break down or a building becomes overcrowded, even going in and out can turn into a hassle. In the same way, families with children or elderly residents may find it harder to enjoy open space or feel truly relaxed when they are living several dozen floors above street level.
components.chinese: 例如，如果升降機壞了，或者整棟樓變得過度擠迫，那麼連出入大廈本身都可能變成一件麻煩事。同樣地，有小孩的家庭或有長者的住戶，也可能會覺得較難享受戶外空間，或者較難在住在幾十層高樓之上時真正放鬆下來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if the lifts break down or a building becomes
overcrowded, even going in and out can turn into a hassle. In the same way,
families with children or elderly residents may find it harder to enjoy open space
or feel truly relaxed when they are living several dozen floors above street level.
[例如，如果升降機壞了，或者整棟樓變得過度擠迫，那麼連出入大廈本身都可能變成
一件麻煩事。同樣地，有小孩的家庭或有長者的住戶，也可能會覺得較難享受戶外空
間，或者較難在住在幾十層高樓之上時真正放鬆下來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say living in tall buildings has both clear strengths and clear drawbacks. It offers convenience and efficiency on the one hand, but on the other hand, it can also make life feel more crowded, more dependent, and a little less human.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說居住在高樓大廈既有明顯的優勢，也有明顯的缺點。一方面，它帶來便利和效率；但另一方面，它也可能令生活變得更擠迫、更依賴系統，而且少了一點人情味。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say living in tall buildings has both clear strengths
and clear drawbacks. It offers convenience and efficiency on the one hand, but
on the other hand, it can also make life feel more crowded, more dependent, and
a little less human.
[總結來說，我會說居住在高樓大廈既有明顯的優勢，也有明顯的缺點。一方面，它帶
來便利和效率；但另一方面，它也可能令生活變得更擠迫、更依賴系統，而且少了一點
人情味。]

question.english: Do you think there will be more tall buildings in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來會有更多高樓大廈嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think there will be more tall buildings in the future?
你認為未來會有更多高樓大廈嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think there will be more tall buildings in the future, especially in major cities where land is limited and demand keeps growing.
components.chinese: 是的，我確實認為未來會有更多高樓大廈，尤其是在土地有限而需求又持續增加的大城市裡。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think there will be more tall buildings in the future, especially
in major cities where land is limited and demand keeps growing.
[是的，我確實認為未來會有更多高樓大廈，尤其是在土地有限而需求又持續增加的
大城市裡。]

components.english: The main reason is that urban populations are still rising in many places, while available land is not. When a city runs out of room to spread outward, the most practical option is often to build upward. In other words, tall buildings are not just a symbol of modernity; they are also a response to pressure. They can provide more homes, more offices, and more facilities without taking up too much ground space.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，很多地方的城市人口仍然在增加，但可用土地卻不會跟著變多。當一座城市已經沒有太多空間向外擴展時，最實際的做法往往就是向上發展。換句話說，高樓不只是現代化的象徵，它同時也是對壓力的一種回應。它們可以在不佔用太多地面空間的情況下，提供更多住宅、辦公室，以及其他設施。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that urban populations are still rising in
many places, while available land is not. When a city runs out of room to spread
outward, the most practical option is often to build upward. In other words,
tall buildings are not just a symbol of modernity; they are also a response to
pressure. They can provide more homes, more offices, and more facilities
without taking up too much ground space.
[主要原因是，很多地方的城市人口仍然在增加，但可用土地卻不會跟著變多。當一座
城市已經沒有太多空間向外擴展時，最實際的做法往往就是向上發展。換句話說，高
樓不只是現代化的象徵，它同時也是對壓力的一種回應。它們可以在不佔用太多地面
空間的情況下，提供更多住宅、辦公室，以及其他設施。]

components.english: For example, in densely populated cities, governments and developers are more likely to build high-rise apartment blocks or office towers instead of low-rise buildings, because that allows far more people to live and work in the same area. It is a bit like folding more function into less land.
components.chinese: 例如，在人口密集的城市裡，政府和發展商通常更傾向興建高層住宅或商業大樓，而不是低層建築，因為這樣可以讓更多人在同一片土地上居住和工作。這某程度上就像把更多功能摺疊進更少的土地裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in densely populated cities, governments and
developers are more likely to build high-rise apartment blocks or office towers
instead of low-rise buildings, because that allows far more people to live and
work in the same area. It is a bit like folding more function into less land.
[例如，在人口密集的城市裡，政府和發展商通常更傾向興建高層住宅或商業大樓，而
不是低層建築，因為這樣可以讓更多人在同一片土地上居住和工作。這某程度上就像
把更多功能摺疊進更少的土地裡。]

components.english: So, overall, I would say the trend towards taller buildings is very likely to continue, because they offer a practical way to deal with population pressure and limited space.
components.chinese: 所以，整體而言，我會說興建更多高樓的趨勢很可能會持續下去，因為它們提供了一種應對人口壓力和土地有限的實際方法。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say the trend towards taller buildings is very
likely to continue, because they offer a practical way to deal with population
pressure and limited space.
[所以，整體而言，我會說興建更多高樓的趨勢很可能會持續下去，因為它們提供了一
種應對人口壓力和土地有限的實際方法。]

components.english: That said, I do not think every place will be filled with skyscrapers, because the future of construction also depends on cost, planning priorities, and local character.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為每一個地方未來都會被摩天大樓填滿，因為建築發展的方向同時也取決於成本、城市規劃重點，以及地方本身的特色。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think every place will be filled with skyscrapers,
because the future of construction also depends on cost, planning priorities, and
local character.
[不過，我也不認為每一個地方未來都會被摩天大樓填滿，因為建築發展的方向同時
也取決於成本、城市規劃重點，以及地方本身的特色。]

components.english: In some cities, building extremely tall structures may be too expensive, too disruptive, or simply unsuitable. Some governments may prefer mid-rise housing, greener neighbourhoods, or stricter heritage protection instead. After all, a city is not just a machine for fitting people in; it is also a place where people are meant to live well. If tall buildings dominate everything, the city may gain height but lose warmth.
components.chinese: 在某些城市裡，興建極高的建築物可能成本太高、干擾太大，或者根本並不合適。有些政府反而可能更傾向發展中層住宅、較綠化的社區，或者更嚴格地保護歷史建築。畢竟，城市不只是用來把人塞進去的機器；它同時也是一個讓人好好生活的地方。如果高樓主導了一切，城市也許是變高了，卻可能失去了溫度。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In some cities, building extremely tall structures may be too
expensive, too disruptive, or simply unsuitable. Some governments may prefer
mid-rise housing, greener neighbourhoods, or stricter heritage protection
instead. After all, a city is not just a machine for fitting people in; it is also a
place where people are meant to live well. If tall buildings dominate everything,
the city may gain height but lose warmth.
[在某些城市裡，興建極高的建築物可能成本太高、干擾太大，或者根本並不合適。有
些政府反而可能更傾向發展中層住宅、較綠化的社區，或者更嚴格地保護歷史建築。
畢竟，城市不只是用來把人塞進去的機器；它同時也是一個讓人好好生活的地方。如
果高樓主導了一切，城市也許是變高了，卻可能失去了溫度。]

components.english: For instance, an older district with historic streets and a strong local identity might benefit more from careful redevelopment than from rows of glass towers. In that case, the smarter choice is not always to build higher, but to build more thoughtfully.
components.chinese: 例如，一個擁有歷史街道和鮮明地方特色的舊區，未必適合興建一排排玻璃高塔，反而可能更適合經過細緻規劃的重建。在這種情況下，更明智的選擇未必是建得更高，而是建得更周到。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, an older district with historic streets and a strong
local identity might benefit more from careful redevelopment than from rows of
glass towers. In that case, the smarter choice is not always to build higher, but to
build more thoughtfully.
[例如，一個擁有歷史街道和鮮明地方特色的舊區，未必適合興建一排排玻璃高塔，反
而可能更適合經過細緻規劃的重建。在這種情況下，更明智的選擇未必是建得更高，
而是建得更周到。]

components.english: Overall, I do think there will be more tall buildings in the future, particularly in crowded urban areas, but I would not say that taller always means better. The best cities will probably be the ones that build upwards when necessary, while still keeping livability, character, and human needs firmly in view.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的確認為未來會有更多高樓大廈，特別是在擠迫的城市地區；不過，我不會認為建得越高就一定越好。真正出色的城市，很可能是那些在有需要時懂得向上發展，但同時仍然把宜居性、地方特色，以及人的需要清楚放在眼前的城市。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I do think there will be more tall buildings in the
future, particularly in crowded urban areas, but I would not say that taller
always means better. The best cities will probably be the ones that build
upwards when necessary, while still keeping livability, character, and human
needs firmly in view.
[總結來說，我的確認為未來會有更多高樓大廈，特別是在擠迫的城市地區；不過，我
不會認為建得越高就一定越好。真正出色的城市，很可能是那些在有需要時懂得向上
發展，但同時仍然把宜居性、地方特色，以及人的需要清楚放在眼前的城市。]

question.english: Why aren’t there many tall buildings in the countryside?
question.chinese: 為甚麼鄉村地區沒有很多高樓大廈？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why aren’t there many tall buildings in the countryside?
為甚麼鄉村地區沒有很多高樓大廈？

components.english: I think the main reason is that there simply is not enough demand in the countryside to justify many tall buildings.
components.chinese: 我認為主要原因是，鄉村地區根本沒有足夠的需求去支持興建大量高樓。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the main reason is that there simply is not enough demand in
the countryside to justify many tall buildings.
[我認為主要原因是，鄉村地區根本沒有足夠的需求去支持興建大量高樓。]

components.english: Rural areas usually have smaller populations, fewer businesses, and far less pressure on land. In a city, space is tight, so buildings tend to rise upwards. In the countryside, however, land is often more available, so people can spread out rather than build up. To put it simply, skyscrapers make the most sense when space is scarce, and that is usually not the case in rural areas.
components.chinese: 鄉村地區通常人口較少、商業活動較少，而且土地壓力也低得多。在城市裡，空間緊張，所以建築物往往會向上發展。不過在鄉村，土地通常較充足，因此人們可以向外擴展，而不是向上堆高。簡單來說，高樓最適合出現在土地稀缺的地方，而這通常不是鄉村地區的情況。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Rural areas usually have smaller populations, fewer businesses,
and far less pressure on land. In a city, space is tight, so buildings tend to rise
upwards. In the countryside, however, land is often more available, so people can
spread out rather than build up. To put it simply, skyscrapers make the most
sense when space is scarce, and that is usually not the case in rural areas.
[鄉村地區通常人口較少、商業活動較少，而且土地壓力也低得多。在城市裡，空間緊
張，所以建築物往往會向上發展。不過在鄉村，土地通常較充足，因此人們可以向外擴
展，而不是向上堆高。簡單來說，高樓最適合出現在土地稀缺的地方，而這通常不是鄉
村地區的情況。]

components.english: For example, in a small village or rural town, people may live in detached houses or low-rise apartment blocks because there is enough land for that kind of development. There is no real need to pack hundreds of families into one tall building when the area is not overcrowded in the first place.
components.chinese: 例如，在一條小村莊或一個鄉鎮裡，人們可能住在獨立屋或低層住宅，因為當地有足夠土地去支持這種發展模式。當一個地方本身並不擠迫時，自然沒有真正需要把幾百戶家庭塞進同一幢高樓裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a small գյուղ村 or rural town, people may live in
detached houses or low-rise apartment blocks because there is enough land for
that kind of development. There is no real need to pack hundreds of families
into one tall building when the area is not overcrowded in the first place.
[例如，在一條小村莊或一個鄉鎮裡，人們可能住在獨立屋或低層住宅，因為當地有足
夠土地去支持這種發展模式。當一個地方本身並不擠迫時，自然沒有真正需要把幾百
戶家庭塞進同一幢高樓裡。]

components.english: So, if I had to sum it up, I would say tall buildings are uncommon in the countryside mainly because the population is lower and the pressure on land is much lighter.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要總結的話，我會說鄉村地區少見高樓，主要是因為人口較少，而且土地壓力也輕得多。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to sum it up, I would say tall buildings are
uncommon in the countryside mainly because the population is lower and the
pressure on land is much lighter.
[所以，如果要總結的話，我會說鄉村地區少見高樓，主要是因為人口較少，而且土地
壓力也輕得多。]

components.english: Another reason is that tall buildings are expensive and often do not match the lifestyle or character of the countryside.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，高樓大廈的建造成本很高，而且往往也不太符合鄉村的生活方式或地方特色。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that tall buildings are expensive and often do not
match the lifestyle or character of the countryside.
[另一個原因是，高樓大廈的建造成本很高，而且往往也不太符合鄉村的生活方式或
地方特色。]

components.english: Building high-rise structures usually requires heavier investment, stronger infrastructure, and more complex planning. You may need better roads, more advanced utilities, lifts, safety systems, and long-term maintenance. In many rural areas, that would be overkill. On top of that, the countryside is often valued for its open views, slower pace of life, and closer connection to nature, so a forest of tall buildings would look a bit out of place.
components.chinese: 興建高樓通常需要更大的投資、更強的基礎設施，以及更複雜的規劃。你可能需要更完善的道路、更先進的公共設施、電梯、安全系統，以及長期保養。在很多鄉村地區，這樣做其實有點太過頭了。除此之外，鄉村之所以吸引人，往往是因為它有開闊的景觀、較慢的生活節奏，以及與大自然更接近的感覺，所以一大片高樓看起來就會有點格格不入。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Building high-rise structures usually requires heavier
investment, stronger infrastructure, and more complex planning. You may need
better roads, more advanced utilities, lifts, safety systems, and long-term
maintenance. In many rural areas, that would be overkill. On top of that, the
countryside is often valued for its open views, slower pace of life, and closer
connection to nature, so a forest of tall buildings would look a bit out of place.
[興建高樓通常需要更大的投資、更強的基礎設施，以及更複雜的規劃。你可能需要更
完善的道路、更先進的公共設施、電梯、安全系統，以及長期保養。在很多鄉村地區，
這樣做其實有點太過頭了。除此之外，鄉村之所以吸引人，往往是因為它有開闊的景
觀、較慢的生活節奏，以及與大自然更接近的感覺，所以一大片高樓看起來就會有點
格格不入。]

components.english: For instance, if a quiet farming area suddenly had several tall towers built in the middle of it, the buildings might block the landscape, change the atmosphere, and even place extra pressure on local roads and services. In that situation, building higher would not necessarily mean living better.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個安靜的農村地區突然在中央建起幾幢高塔，那些建築物可能會遮擋風景、改變整體氣氛，甚至為當地道路和公共服務帶來額外壓力。在那種情況下，建得更高並不一定代表住得更好。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a quiet farming area suddenly had several tall
towers built in the middle of it, the buildings might block the landscape, change
the atmosphere, and even place extra pressure on local roads and services. In that
situation, building higher would not necessarily mean living better.
[例如，如果一個安靜的農村地區突然在中央建起幾幢高塔，那些建築物可能會遮擋
風景、改變整體氣氛，甚至為當地道路和公共服務帶來額外壓力。在那種情況下，建得
更高並不一定代表住得更好。]

components.english: Overall, I would say there are not many tall buildings in the countryside because they are often unnecessary, costly, and out of step with rural life. In a way, the countryside is meant to breathe, not to be boxed in by concrete.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說鄉村地區沒有很多高樓，因為那樣的建築通常既沒有必要，成本又高，而且和鄉村生活並不協調。某程度上，鄉村本來就應該保留那種可以「呼吸」的空間，而不是被水泥叢林框住。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say there are not many tall buildings in the
countryside because they are often unnecessary, costly, and out of step with
rural life. In a way, the countryside is meant to breathe, not to be boxed in by
concrete.
[總結來說，我會說鄉村地區沒有很多高樓，因為那樣的建築通常既沒有必要，成本又
高，而且和鄉村生活並不協調。某程度上，鄉村本來就應該保留那種可以「呼吸」的空
間，而不是被水泥叢林框住。]

question.english: Why do some people like to live in tall buildings nowadays?
question.chinese: 為甚麼現在有些人喜歡住在高樓大廈裡？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people like to live in tall buildings nowadays?
為甚麼現在有些人喜歡住在高樓大廈裡？

components.english: I think one of the main reasons is convenience, because tall buildings are often located in busy urban areas where everything is close at hand.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要原因就是方便，因為高樓大廈通常位於繁忙的市區，很多東西都近在咫尺。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the main reasons is convenience, because tall buildings are
often located in busy urban areas where everything is close at hand.
[我認為，其中一個主要原因就是方便，因為高樓大廈通常位於繁忙的市區，很多東西
都近在咫尺。]

components.english: Nowadays, many people live fast-paced lives, so they want their home to be close to transport, shops, schools, and workplaces. Living in a tall building often makes that possible. In many cities, high-rise living is simply part and parcel of modern life, especially where land is scarce. In that sense, a tall building can feel like a vertical community, with daily necessities right on your doorstep.
components.chinese: 現代很多人生活節奏很快，所以他們希望自己的住處靠近交通、商店、學校，以及工作地點。住在高樓大廈裡，往往就能做到這一點。在很多城市裡，高層住宅本身就已經成為現代生活不可分割的一部分，尤其是在土地短缺的地方。從這個角度來看，一棟高樓有時就像一個垂直社區，日常所需幾乎都在家門口。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Nowadays, many people live fast-paced lives, so they want their
home to be close to transport, shops, schools, and workplaces. Living in a tall
building often makes that possible. In many cities, high-rise living is simply part
and parcel of modern life, especially where land is scarce. In that sense, a tall
building can feel like a vertical community, with daily necessities right on your
doorstep.
[現代很多人生活節奏很快，所以他們希望自己的住處靠近交通、商店、學校，以及工
作地點。住在高樓大廈裡，往往就能做到這一點。在很多城市裡，高層住宅本身就已經
成為現代生活不可分割的一部分，尤其是在土地短缺的地方。從這個角度來看，一棟
高樓有時就像一個垂直社區，日常所需幾乎都在家門口。]

components.english: For example, a person living in a high-rise apartment may have the subway downstairs, a supermarket nearby, and a short commute to work. For busy people, that kind of setup can save both time and energy.
components.chinese: 例如，一個住在高層住宅的人，可能樓下就是地鐵站，附近就有超市，而且上班時間也大大縮短。對生活忙碌的人來說，這樣的安排確實能節省很多時間和精力。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a person living in a high-rise apartment may have the
subway downstairs, a supermarket nearby, and a short commute to work. For
busy people, that kind of setup can save both time and energy.
[例如，一個住在高層住宅的人，可能樓下就是地鐵站，附近就有超市，而且上班時間
也大大縮短。對生活忙碌的人來說，這樣的安排確實能節省很多時間和精力。]

components.english: So, one clear reason people like tall buildings is that they make everyday life more efficient and manageable.
components.chinese: 所以，人們喜歡高樓大廈的一個很明顯原因，就是它能令日常生活變得更有效率，也更容易應付。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear reason people like tall buildings is that they make
everyday life more efficient and manageable.
[所以，人們喜歡高樓大廈的一個很明顯原因，就是它能令日常生活變得更有效率，也
更容易應付。]

components.english: Another reason is that some people are attracted by the lifestyle, the views, and the sense of modern comfort that tall buildings can offer.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，有些人會被那種生活方式、開闊景觀，以及高樓大廈所帶來的現代舒適感吸引。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that some people are attracted by the lifestyle, the
views, and the sense of modern comfort that tall buildings can offer.
[另一個原因是，有些人會被那種生活方式、開闊景觀，以及高樓大廈所帶來的現代舒
適感吸引。]

components.english: To be honest, living high above the ground can feel quite appealing. Some people enjoy the privacy, the security, and the feeling of being slightly removed from the noise and chaos of the street below. A high-floor flat can also offer more light, a better view, and sometimes a touch of status as well. In a way, it allows people to rise above the rush, at least when they go home at the end of the day.
components.chinese: 老實說，住在高高的樓層上，對很多人來說確實很有吸引力。有些人喜歡那種私隱度、安全感，以及和樓下街道的喧鬧與混亂稍微保持距離的感覺。高層單位通常也能帶來更充足的光線、更好的景觀，有時甚至還帶一點身份象徵。某程度上，它讓人回到家時，能夠暫時從繁忙的生活裡抽離出來。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, living high above the ground can feel quite
appealing. Some people enjoy the privacy, the security, and the feeling of being
slightly removed from the noise and chaos of the street below. A high-floor flat
can also offer more light, a better view, and sometimes a touch of status as well.
In a way, it allows people to rise above the rush, at least when they go home at
the end of the day.
[老實說，住在高高的樓層上，對很多人來說確實很有吸引力。有些人喜歡那種私隱
度、安全感，以及和樓下街道的喧鬧與混亂稍微保持距離的感覺。高層單位通常也能
帶來更充足的光線、更好的景觀，有時甚至還帶一點身份象徵。某程度上，它讓人回到
家時，能夠暫時從繁忙的生活裡抽離出來。]

components.english: For instance, some residents love coming home to a flat with a wide city view, especially at night when the skyline is lit up. Others may value the facilities that often come with modern high-rise buildings, such as security systems, clubhouses, gyms, or shared gardens.
components.chinese: 例如，有些住戶很喜歡回到家時看見廣闊的城市景觀，尤其是在夜晚燈火通明的時候。也有些人很重視現代高層住宅常見的配套設施，例如保安系統、會所、健身室，或者共用花園。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some residents love coming home to a flat with a wide
city view, especially at night when the skyline is lit up. Others may value the
facilities that often come with modern high-rise buildings, such as security
systems, clubhouses, gyms, or shared gardens.
[例如，有些住戶很喜歡回到家時看見廣闊的城市景觀，尤其是在夜晚燈火通明的時
候。也有些人很重視現代高層住宅常見的配套設施，例如保安系統、會所、健身室，或
者共用花園。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people like living in tall buildings nowadays because they combine practicality with a certain lifestyle appeal. They offer convenience on the one hand, and comfort, views, and modern facilities on the other.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現代人喜歡住在高樓大廈裡，是因為它把實際便利和某種生活吸引力結合在一起。一方面，它提供方便；另一方面，它也帶來舒適感、景觀，以及現代化設施。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people like living in tall buildings nowadays
because they combine practicality with a certain lifestyle appeal. They offer
convenience on the one hand, and comfort, views, and modern facilities on the
other.
[總結來說，我會說現代人喜歡住在高樓大廈裡，是因為它把實際便利和某種生活吸
引力結合在一起。一方面，它提供方便；另一方面，它也帶來舒適感、景觀，以及現代
化設施。]

question.english: Is it true that tall buildings are more beneficial than small buildings?
question.chinese: 高樓大廈是否真的比小型建築更有好處？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it true that tall buildings are more beneficial than small buildings?
高樓大廈是否真的比小型建築更有好處？

components.english: I would not say that is always true, because whether tall buildings are more beneficial really depends on the needs of a particular city.
components.chinese: 我不會說這一定是真的，因為高樓是否更有好處，其實很大程度上取決於某一座城市本身的需要。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I would not say that is always true, because whether tall buildings are
more beneficial really depends on the needs of a particular city.
[我不會說這一定是真的，因為高樓是否更有好處，其實很大程度上取決於某一座城
市本身的需要。]

components.english: In some places, tall buildings clearly have strong advantages. When land is limited and population density is high, building upwards can be the most practical solution. Tall buildings can provide more homes, more office space, and more facilities without swallowing up too much land. In that sense, they can help a city make more out of less. That said, height by itself is not a magic wand. If towers are badly planned, they can create congestion, put pressure on transport and public services, and make urban life feel more cramped rather than more comfortable.
components.chinese: 在某些地方，高樓的確有很明顯的優勢。當土地有限，而人口密度又很高時，向上發展往往是最實際的解決方法。高樓可以在不大量吞噬土地的情況下，提供更多住宅、辦公空間，以及其他設施。從這個角度來看，它能幫助城市用更少的土地做到更多事情。不過，單單「高」本身並不是萬能藥。如果規劃做得不好，高樓也可能造成擠塞、加重交通和公共服務的壓力，並令城市生活變得更侷促，而不是更舒適。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In some places, tall buildings clearly have strong advantages.
When land is limited and population density is high, building upwards can be
the most practical solution. Tall buildings can provide more homes, more office
space, and more facilities without swallowing up too much land. In that sense,
they can help a city make more out of less. That said, height by itself is not a
magic wand. If towers are badly planned, they can create congestion, put
pressure on transport and public services, and make urban life feel more
cramped rather than more comfortable.
[在某些地方，高樓的確有很明顯的優勢。當土地有限，而人口密度又很高時，向上發
展往往是最實際的解決方法。高樓可以在不大量吞噬土地的情況下，提供更多住宅、
辦公空間，以及其他設施。從這個角度來看，它能幫助城市用更少的土地做到更多事
情。不過，單單「高」本身並不是萬能藥。如果規劃做得不好，高樓也可能造成擠塞、加
重交通和公共服務的壓力，並令城市生活變得更侷促，而不是更舒適。]

components.english: For example, in a crowded city centre, high-rise residential blocks may be extremely useful because they allow thousands of people to live near jobs, transport links, and daily services. Without that kind of vertical development, the city might sprawl endlessly or push housing even further out of reach.
components.chinese: 例如，在擠迫的市中心，高層住宅大樓可能非常有用，因為它們能讓成千上萬的人住在工作地點、交通網絡，以及日常服務附近。若沒有這種向上發展的方式，城市可能會無止境地向外擴張，或者令住屋變得更加難以負擔。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a crowded city centre, high-rise residential blocks
may be extremely useful because they allow thousands of people to live near jobs,
transport links, and daily services. Without that kind of vertical development,
the city might sprawl endlessly or push housing even further out of reach.
[例如，在擠迫的市中心，高層住宅大樓可能非常有用，因為它們能讓成千上萬的人住
在工作地點、交通網絡，以及日常服務附近。若沒有這種向上發展的方式，城市可能會
無止境地向外擴張，或者令住屋變得更加難以負擔。]

components.english: So, in high-density areas, tall buildings can certainly be more beneficial, but only when they are supported by smart planning and strong infrastructure.
components.chinese: 所以，在高密度地區，高樓確實可以更有好處，但前提是它們要有完善的規劃和穩健的基建配合。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in high-density areas, tall buildings can certainly be more
beneficial, but only when they are supported by smart planning and strong
infrastructure.
[所以，在高密度地區，高樓確實可以更有好處，但前提是它們要有完善的規劃和穩健
的基建配合。]

components.english: On the other hand, small buildings can sometimes be more beneficial because they often create a more human-scale and livable environment.
components.chinese: 另一方面，小型建築有時反而可能更有好處，因為它們往往能營造一種更貼近人的尺度、也更宜居的生活環境。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On the other hand, small buildings can sometimes be more beneficial
because they often create a more human-scale and livable environment.
[另一方面，小型建築有時反而可能更有好處，因為它們往往能營造一種更貼近人的
尺度、也更宜居的生活環境。]

components.english: Low-rise areas are often easier to navigate, less visually overwhelming, and more closely connected to neighbourhood life. They can preserve local character, allow more sunlight into the streets, and make communities feel less anonymous. A city full of towers may look impressive on paper, but if it starts to feel like a forest of concrete, something important can be lost. Sometimes smaller buildings give a place more warmth, more identity, and more breathing room.
components.chinese: 低層建築區通常更容易穿梭，不會在視覺上帶來那麼強的壓迫感，而且與社區生活的連結也往往更緊密。它們可以保留地方特色，讓街道有更多陽光，也令社區不會那麼陌生冷淡。一座滿是高塔的城市，紙面上看起來也許很壯觀，但如果它開始讓人感覺像一片鋼筋水泥森林，那就可能失去一些很重要的東西。有時候，小型建築反而能帶來更多溫度、更多個性，以及更多喘息空間。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Low-rise areas are often easier to navigate, less visually
overwhelming, and more closely connected to neighbourhood life. They can
preserve local character, allow more sunlight into the streets, and make
communities feel less anonymous. A city full of towers may look impressive on
paper, but if it starts to feel like a forest of concrete, something important can
be lost. Sometimes smaller buildings give a place more warmth, more identity,
and more breathing room.
[低層建築區通常更容易穿梭，不會在視覺上帶來那麼強的壓迫感，而且與社區生活
的連結也往往更緊密。它們可以保留地方特色，讓街道有更多陽光，也令社區不會那
麼陌生冷淡。一座滿是高塔的城市，紙面上看起來也許很壯觀，但如果它開始讓人感
覺像一片鋼筋水泥森林，那就可能失去一些很重要的東西。有時候，小型建築反而能
帶來更多溫度、更多個性，以及更多喘息空間。]

components.english: For instance, a low-rise neighbourhood with small shops, schools, parks, and mid-rise housing may feel far more welcoming than an area dominated by huge towers. People may be more likely to walk, interact with neighbours, and feel attached to the place where they live.
components.chinese: 例如，一個有小商店、學校、公園，以及中低層住宅的社區，往往會比一個被巨大高樓主導的地區更令人感到親切。在那樣的環境中，人們可能更願意步行、更容易和鄰里互動，也更容易對自己居住的地方產生歸屬感。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a low-rise neighbourhood with small shops, schools,
parks, and mid-rise housing may feel far more welcoming than an area
dominated by huge towers. People may be more likely to walk, interact with
neighbours, and feel attached to the place where they live.
[例如，一個有小商店、學校、公園，以及中低層住宅的社區，往往會比一個被巨大高
樓主導的地區更令人感到親切。在那樣的環境中，人們可能更願意步行、更容易和鄰
里互動，也更容易對自己居住的地方產生歸屬感。]

components.english: Overall, I would not say tall buildings are automatically more beneficial than small buildings. Tall buildings work well when a city needs efficiency and density, whereas small buildings often shine when livability, community, and local character matter more. So, to me, the best answer is not “the taller, the better,” but “the right building for the right place.”
components.chinese: 總結來說，我不會說高樓一定比小型建築更有好處。當一座城市需要效率和高密度發展時，高樓會發揮很大作用；而當宜居性、社區感，以及地方特色更重要時，小型建築往往更有優勢。所以，在我看來，最好的答案不是「越高越好」，而是「合適的地方，配合合適的建築」。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would not say tall buildings are automatically more
beneficial than small buildings. Tall buildings work well when a city needs
efficiency and density, whereas small buildings often shine when livability,
community, and local character matter more. So, to me, the best answer is not
“the taller, the better,” but “the right building for the right place.”
[總結來說，我不會說高樓一定比小型建築更有好處。當一座城市需要效率和高密度
發展時，高樓會發揮很大作用；而當宜居性、社區感，以及地方特色更重要時，小型建
築往往更有優勢。所以，在我看來，最好的答案不是「越高越好」，而是「合適的地方，
配合合適的建築」。]

question.english: Why do a lot of people enjoy going to crowded places?
question.chinese: 為甚麼很多人喜歡去人多擠迫的地方？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do a lot of people enjoy going to crowded places?
[為甚麼很多人喜歡去人多擠迫的地方？]

components.english: I think one major reason is that crowded places often feel lively, exciting, and full of energy, so people are naturally drawn to that kind of atmosphere.
components.chinese: 我認為其中一個主要原因是，人多的地方往往感覺熱鬧、有活力，而且充滿氣氛，所以人們自然會被這種環境吸引。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major reason is that crowded places often feel lively,
exciting, and full of energy, so people are naturally drawn to that kind of
atmosphere.
[我認為其中一個主要原因是，人多的地方往往感覺熱鬧、有活力，而且充滿氣氛，所
以人們自然會被這種環境吸引。]

components.english: Human beings are social by nature, so many people enjoy being where the action is rather than sitting somewhere that feels flat or lifeless. A crowded place usually has a certain buzz about it. When there are lots of people talking, laughing, shopping, or celebrating, the mood can become almost contagious. In that sense, the crowd itself becomes part of the attraction, because it makes the place feel more alive and more memorable.
components.chinese: 人本身就是群體性的動物，所以很多人都喜歡待在熱鬧、有事情發生的地方，而不是待在一個平淡、冷清的環境裡。人多的地方通常都會有一種特別的熱鬧感。當很多人在聊天、歡笑、購物，或者一起慶祝時，那種氣氛往往會變得像會感染人一樣。從這個角度來看，人群本身就成了吸引力的一部分，因為它會令一個地方顯得更有生命力，也更令人難忘。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Human beings are social by nature, so many people enjoy being
where the action is rather than sitting somewhere that feels flat or lifeless. A
crowded place usually has a certain buzz about it. When there are lots of people
talking, laughing, shopping, or celebrating, the mood can become almost
contagious. In that sense, the crowd itself becomes part of the attraction,
because it makes the place feel more alive and more memorable.
[人本身就是群體性的動物，所以很多人都喜歡待在熱鬧、有事情發生的地方，而不是
待在一個平淡、冷清的環境裡。人多的地方通常都會有一種特別的熱鬧感。當很多人
在聊天、歡笑、購物，或者一起慶祝時，那種氣氛往往會變得像會感染人一樣。從這個
角度來看，人群本身就成了吸引力的一部分，因為它會令一個地方顯得更有生命力，
也更令人難忘。]

components.english: For example, people often enjoy going to night markets, festivals, concerts, or busy shopping streets, because those places are humming with life. Even if they do not buy very much or stay very long, they may still enjoy soaking up the atmosphere and feeling part of something bigger than themselves.
components.chinese: 例如，人們往往喜歡去夜市、節慶活動、演唱會，或者繁忙的購物街，因為那些地方充滿生氣。即使他們未必買很多東西，或者未必逗留很久，他們仍然可能很享受那種氣氛，並感受到自己像是某個更大場景中的一部分。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people often enjoy going to night markets, festivals,
concerts, or busy shopping streets, because those places are humming with life.
Even if they do not buy very much or stay very long, they may still enjoy soaking
up the atmosphere and feeling part of something bigger than themselves.
[例如，人們往往喜歡去夜市、節慶活動、演唱會，或者繁忙的購物街，因為那些地方
充滿生氣。即使他們未必買很多東西，或者未必逗留很久，他們仍然可能很享受那種
氣氛，並感受到自己像是某個更大場景中的一部分。]

components.english: So, from my point of view, many people enjoy crowded places because they offer energy, excitement, and a stronger sense of connection.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，很多人喜歡去人多的地方，是因為那裡能帶來活力、刺激感，以及更強的連結感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from my point of view, many people enjoy crowded places
because they offer energy, excitement, and a stronger sense of connection.
[所以，在我看來，很多人喜歡去人多的地方，是因為那裡能帶來活力、刺激感，以及
更強的連結感。]

components.english: Another reason is that busy places often seem more attractive, because people tend to associate crowds with popularity, entertainment, and better opportunities to socialise.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，熱鬧的地方通常會顯得更有吸引力，因為人們往往會把人潮和受歡迎、好玩，以及更多社交機會聯繫在一起。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that busy places often seem more attractive, because
people tend to associate crowds with popularity, entertainment, and better
opportunities to socialise.
[另一個原因是，熱鬧的地方通常會顯得更有吸引力，因為人們往往會把人潮和受歡
迎、好玩，以及更多社交機會聯繫在一起。]

components.english: In many cases, if a place is packed, people assume there must be a good reason for it. They may see it as a sign that the food is better, the event is more exciting, or the overall experience is more worthwhile. On top of that, crowded places make it easier to meet friends, people-watch, or simply avoid the feeling of being alone. For some people, a crowd is not a nuisance; it is social proof that they are in the right place.
components.chinese: 在很多情況下，如果一個地方擠滿了人，人們就會覺得那一定是有原因的。他們可能會把這看成一種訊號，表示那裡的食物更好、活動更精彩，或者整體體驗更值得。除此之外，人多的地方也更容易讓人和朋友見面、觀察不同的人，或者單純避免那種孤單感。對某些人來說，人群不是麻煩，反而是一種「這裡值得來」的證明。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In many cases, if a place is packed, people assume there must be
a good reason for it. They may see it as a sign that the food is better, the event is
more exciting, or the overall experience is more worthwhile. On top of that,
crowded places make it easier to meet friends, people-watch, or simply avoid the
feeling of being alone. For some people, a crowd is not a nuisance; it is social
proof that they are in the right place.
[在很多情況下，如果一個地方擠滿了人，人們就會覺得那一定是有原因的。他們可能
會把這看成一種訊號，表示那裡的食物更好、活動更精彩，或者整體體驗更值得。除此
之外，人多的地方也更容易讓人和朋友見面、觀察不同的人，或者單純避免那種孤單
感。對某些人來說，人群不是麻煩，反而是一種「這裡值得來」的證明。]

components.english: For instance, if two restaurants are side by side and one is empty while the other has a queue outside, many people will instinctively choose the busy one. The same goes for tourist attractions, cafés, and weekend markets: a crowd often creates the impression that the place is worth checking out.
components.chinese: 例如，如果兩間餐廳並排在一起，一間空空的，另一間外面排滿隊，很多人都會本能地選擇那間多人排隊的。旅遊景點、咖啡店和週末市集也是一樣：人潮往往會讓人覺得那個地方值得去看看。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if two restaurants are side by side and one is empty
while the other has a queue outside, many people will instinctively choose the
busy one. The same goes for tourist attractions, cafés, and weekend markets: a
crowd often creates the impression that the place is worth checking out.
[例如，如果兩間餐廳並排在一起，一間空空的，另一間外面排滿隊，很多人都會本能
地選擇那間多人排隊的。旅遊景點、咖啡店和週末市集也是一樣：人潮往往會讓人覺
得那個地方值得去看看。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a lot of people enjoy crowded places because they crave both atmosphere and human connection. That said, it still depends on personality, because what feels electric to one person may feel overwhelming to another.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說很多人喜歡去人多的地方，是因為他們同時渴望氣氛和人與人之間的連結。不過，這仍然取決於個人性格，因為對某些人來說充滿活力的環境，對另一些人來說卻可能太令人吃不消。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a lot of people enjoy crowded places because
they crave both atmosphere and human connection. That said, it still depends
on personality, because what feels electric to one person may feel overwhelming
to another.
[總結來說，我會說很多人喜歡去人多的地方，是因為他們同時渴望氣氛和人與人之
間的連結。不過，這仍然取決於個人性格，因為對某些人來說充滿活力的環境，對另一
些人來說卻可能太令人吃不消。]

question.english: Where (or what) would you say is the most crowded place in your city?
question.chinese: 你會說你城市裡最擠迫的地方是甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: Where (or what) would you say is the most crowded place in your city?
你會說你城市裡最擠迫的地方是甚麼？

components.english: If I had to name one place in Hong Kong, I would probably say Mong Kok.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我在香港說出一個地方，我大概會選旺角。

source_lines.text: 1. If I had to name one place in Hong Kong, I would probably say Mong Kok.
[如果一定要我在香港說出一個地方，我大概會選旺角。]

components.english: The main reason is that Mong Kok has long had a reputation for being dense, busy, and packed with people. Even the name “Mong Kok” is often linked to the idea of a “crowded corner”, and the area is widely known as one of the busiest commercial districts in Kowloon. On top of that, the wider Yau Tsim Mong district has high population density and large numbers of transient visitors, especially tourists and shoppers, so the whole area is rarely quiet.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，旺角一直以來都給人一種人口密集、節奏繁忙，而且人潮洶湧的印象。就連「旺角」這個名字本身，也常常和「擠迫的角落」這種概念聯繫在一起，而那一帶也一直被視為九龍其中一個最繁忙的商業區。除此之外，更廣泛的油尖旺區本身人口密度也很高，而且有大量流動人口，特別是遊客和購物人士，所以整個地區幾乎很少真正安靜下來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that Mong Kok has long had a reputation
for being dense, busy, and packed with people. Even the name “Mong Kok” is
often linked to the idea of a “crowded corner”, and the area is widely known as
one of the busiest commercial districts in Kowloon. On top of that, the wider
Yau Tsim Mong district has high population density and large numbers of
transient visitors, especially tourists and shoppers, so the whole area is rarely
quiet.
[主要原因是，旺角一直以來都給人一種人口密集、節奏繁忙，而且人潮洶湧的印象。
就連「旺角」這個名字本身，也常常和「擠迫的角落」這種概念聯繫在一起，而那一帶也
一直被視為九龍其中一個最繁忙的商業區。除此之外，更廣泛的油尖旺區本身人口密
度也很高，而且有大量流動人口，特別是遊客和購物人士，所以整個地區幾乎很少真
正安靜下來。]

components.english: For example, once you get near the shopping streets and market areas in Mong Kok, the place can feel like a river of people. Areas around the famous Ladies’ Market are especially lively, because the market stretches for about a kilometre, has over 100 stalls, and is known to buzz with activity in the afternoons and evenings.
components.chinese: 例如，當你走近旺角的購物街和市集區時，那個地方真的會讓人感覺像一條人流不斷向前湧動的河。尤其是女人街附近，氣氛特別熱鬧，因為那個市集本身綿延大約一公里，有超過一百個攤檔，而且官方旅遊介紹也形容那裡在下午和晚間總是充滿活動與人潮。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, once you get near the shopping streets and market
areas in Mong Kok, the place can feel like a river of people. Areas around the
famous Ladies’ Market are especially lively, because the market stretches for
about a kilometre, has over 100 stalls, and is known to buzz with activity in the
afternoons and evenings.
[例如，當你走近旺角的購物街和市集區時，那個地方真的會讓人感覺像一條人流不
斷向前湧動的河。尤其是女人街附近，氣氛特別熱鬧，因為那個市集本身綿延大約一
公里，有超過一百個攤檔，而且官方旅遊介紹也形容那裡在下午和晚間總是充滿活動
與人潮。]

components.english: So, if the question is about one specific district, Mong Kok is the first place that springs to mind.
components.chinese: 所以，如果問題是指一個具體地區的話，旺角絕對是我第一時間想到的地方。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if the question is about one specific district, Mong Kok is the
first place that springs to mind.
[所以，如果問題是指一個具體地區的話，旺角絕對是我第一時間想到的地方。]

components.english: More specifically, I would say the area around Mong Kok station and the nearby street markets feels even more crowded than the district in general.
components.chinese: 再具體一點來說，我會說旺角站附近，以及周邊街市和露天市集一帶，給人的擠迫感甚至比整個區域的平均感受更強。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: More specifically, I would say the area around Mong Kok station and
the nearby street markets feels even more crowded than the district in general.
[再具體一點來說，我會說旺角站附近，以及周邊街市和露天市集一帶，給人的擠迫感
甚至比整個區域的平均感受更強。]

components.english: I think that is because several things collide there at once: transport, shopping, tourism, and everyday local life. When all of those streams meet in one place, the streets can feel as though the city has been turned up to full volume. It is lively, exciting, and full of character, but it can also be a little overwhelming if you are not used to that pace.
components.chinese: 我認為那是因為很多元素都在那裡同時碰在一起：交通、人流、購物、旅遊，以及本地人的日常生活。當這幾股人流在同一個地方交匯時，整條街就會讓人感覺像是整座城市都被調到最大音量一樣。它很熱鬧、很有活力，也很有性格，但如果你不習慣那種節奏，也確實可能會覺得有點吃不消。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think that is because several things collide there at once:
transport, shopping, tourism, and everyday local life. When all of those streams
meet in one place, the streets can feel as though the city has been turned up to
full volume. It is lively, exciting, and full of character, but it can also be a little
overwhelming if you are not used to that pace.
[我認為那是因為很多元素都在那裡同時碰在一起：交通、人流、購物、旅遊，以及本地
人的日常生活。當這幾股人流在同一個地方交匯時，整條街就會讓人感覺像是整座城
市都被調到最大音量一樣。它很熱鬧、很有活力，也很有性格，但如果你不習慣那種節
奏，也確實可能會覺得有點吃不消。]

components.english: For instance, if you walk through the Ladies’ Market area in the late afternoon or evening, with stalls on both sides and crowds moving shoulder to shoulder, you can really see why Mong Kok has that reputation. It is the sort of place where the city seems to come at you all at once.
components.chinese: 例如，如果你在傍晚或晚上的時候走過女人街一帶，兩旁都是攤檔，人群又幾乎肩並肩地往前移動，你就會很明白為甚麼旺角會有這樣的名聲。那是一種整座城市彷彿一下子朝你湧過來的地方。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if you walk through the Ladies’ Market area in the
late afternoon or evening, with stalls on both sides and crowds moving shoulder
to shoulder, you can really see why Mong Kok has that reputation. It is the sort
of place where the city seems to come at you all at once.
[例如，如果你在傍晚或晚上的時候走過女人街一帶，兩旁都是攤檔，人群又幾乎肩並
肩地往前移動，你就會很明白為甚麼旺角會有這樣的名聲。那是一種整座城市彷彿一
下子朝你湧過來的地方。]

components.english: Overall, if we are talking about Hong Kong, I would say Mong Kok takes the cake. It is probably the place that best captures the crowded, fast-moving, high-energy side of the city.
components.chinese: 總結來說，如果我們談的是香港，我會說旺角最能代表「最擠迫」這個印象。它大概也是最能體現這座城市那種擠迫、快速而充滿能量的一面的地方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, if we are talking about Hong Kong, I would say Mong
Kok takes the cake. It is probably the place that best captures the crowded,
fast-moving, high-energy side of the city.
[總結來說，如果我們談的是香港，我會說旺角最能代表「最擠迫」這個印象。它大概也
是最能體現這座城市那種擠迫、快速而充滿能量的一面的地方。]

question.english: Is it important for a city to have amusement or recreational facilities?
question.chinese: 城市擁有娛樂或康樂設施是否重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it important for a city to have amusement or recreational facilities?
城市擁有娛樂或康樂設施是否重要？

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think it is very important, because a city should be more than just a place where people work, commute, and go home exhausted.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對重要。我認為這非常重要，因為一座城市不應只是讓人工作、通勤，然後筋疲力盡回家的地方。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think it is very important, because a city should be
more than just a place where people work, commute, and go home exhausted.
[是的，絕對重要。我認為這非常重要，因為一座城市不應只是讓人工作、通勤，然後
筋疲力盡回家的地方。]

components.english: Amusement and recreational facilities, such as parks, sports centres, playgrounds, cinemas, and public leisure spaces, play a vital role in people’s quality of life. They give residents a chance to unwind, stay active, and take a break from the pressure of urban living. Without them, city life can start to feel a bit like a treadmill: people keep moving, but they do not necessarily feel better.
