components.chinese: 當人還在成長階段時，身體和大腦其實在背後進行大量工作，所以休息並不是奢侈，而是健康發展的一部分。再加上，很多青少年在晚上未必會很早自然感到睏，因此「早點睡」很多時候都是說起來容易，做起來難。所以，如果他們睡得晚，同時又仍然需要很多休息，那麼他們很難一大清早就精神十足地起床，其實一點也不奇怪。從這個角度來說，遲起床很多時候與其說是懶惰，不如說更關乎生理因素。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people are still growing, their bodies and brains are doing
a huge amount of work behind the scenes, so rest is not a luxury; it is part of
healthy development. On top of that, many teenagers do not naturally feel
sleepy very early at night, which means going to bed early can feel easier said
than done. So if they sleep late and still need plenty of rest, it is no surprise that
they struggle to bounce out of bed at the crack of dawn. In that sense, waking
up late is often less about laziness and more about biology.
[當人還在成長階段時，身體和大腦其實在背後進行大量工作，所以休息並不是奢侈，而是
健康發展的一部分。再加上，很多青少年在晚上未必會很早自然感到睏，因此「早點睡」很多
時候都是說起來容易，做起來難。所以，如果他們睡得晚，同時又仍然需要很多休息，那麼
他們很難一大清早就精神十足地起床，其實一點也不奇怪。從這個角度來說，遲起床很多時
候與其說是懶惰，不如說更關乎生理因素。]

components.english: For example, after a long week of school, homework, and activities, many teenagers will sleep in at the weekend and wake up much later than their parents. That is often their way of catching up on sleep and giving their body a chance to recover.
components.chinese: 例如，經過一整個星期的上學、做功課，以及參加各種活動之後，很多青少年到了週末都會睡到比較晚，起床時間也比父母遲得多。這往往是他們補回睡眠，以及讓身體有機會恢復的一種方式。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, after a long week of school, homework, and activities,
many teenagers will sleep in at the weekend and wake up much later than their
parents. That is often their way of catching up on sleep and giving their body a
chance to recover.
[例如，經過一整個星期的上學、做功課，以及參加各種活動之後，很多青少年到了週末都
會睡到比較晚，起床時間也比父母遲得多。這往往是他們補回睡眠，以及讓身體有機會恢復
的一種方式。]

components.english: So, from my point of view, children and youngsters often wake up late because they genuinely need the rest, and their natural sleep rhythm does not always match an early start.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，小孩子和年輕人之所以常常較遲起床，是因為他們的確需要那些休息，而且他們自然的睡眠節奏也未必和早起的生活方式完全配合。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from my point of view, children and youngsters often wake
up late because they genuinely need the rest, and their natural sleep rhythm
does not always match an early start.
[所以，在我看來，小孩子和年輕人之所以常常較遲起床，是因為他們的確需要那些休息，
而且他們自然的睡眠節奏也未必和早起的生活方式完全配合。]

components.english: That said, lifestyle also plays a big part, because many young people stay up late due to study pressure, screen time, or simply wanting a bit of freedom at the end of the day.
components.chinese: 不過，生活方式也佔了很大原因，因為很多年輕人會因為學業壓力、使用電子產品，或者只是想在一天結束前擁有一點屬於自己的時間，而很晚才睡。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, lifestyle also plays a big part, because many young people
stay up late due to study pressure, screen time, or simply wanting a bit of
freedom at the end of the day.
[不過，生活方式也佔了很大原因，因為很多年輕人會因為學業壓力、使用電子產品，或者
只是想在一天結束前擁有一點屬於自己的時間，而很晚才睡。]

components.english: These days, young people often have packed schedules, so nighttime becomes the only part of the day that feels like their own. Some are finishing homework, some are scrolling through their phones, and some are just trying to switch off after a stressful day. Before they know it, the clock has run away with them. If they go to bed too late, waking up early the next morning can feel like dragging themselves through mud. So in many cases, late mornings are not just a sleep issue; they are also a habit issue.
components.chinese: 如今，年輕人的日程往往排得很滿，所以晚上反而成了他們一天之中唯一真正屬於自己的時間。有些人在做功課，有些人在滑手機，也有些人只是想在壓力很大的一天之後讓自己放鬆下來。等他們回過神來，時間往往已經不知不覺溜走了。如果他們睡得太晚，第二天要早起就會像拖著沉重的身體在泥地裡走一樣辛苦。所以在很多情況下，遲起不只是睡眠問題，也是一種生活習慣問題。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, young people often have packed schedules, so
nighttime becomes the only part of the day that feels like their own. Some are
finishing homework, some are scrolling through their phones, and some are just
trying to switch off after a stressful day. Before they know it, the clock has run
away with them. If they go to bed too late, waking up early the next morning
can feel like dragging themselves through mud. So in many cases, late mornings
are not just a sleep issue; they are also a habit issue.
[如今，年輕人的日程往往排得很滿，所以晚上反而成了他們一天之中唯一真正屬於自己的
時間。有些人在做功課，有些人在滑手機，也有些人只是想在壓力很大的一天之後讓自己放
鬆下來。等他們回過神來，時間往往已經不知不覺溜走了。如果他們睡得太晚，第二天要早
起就會像拖著沉重的身體在泥地裡走一樣辛苦。所以在很多情況下，遲起不只是睡眠問題，
也是一種生活習慣問題。]

components.english: For instance, a secondary school student might stay up revising for a test, then spend another hour watching videos or chatting with friends. If they do that regularly, waking up early becomes an uphill battle, especially on days when they do not have to rush out of the house.
components.chinese: 例如，一個中學生可能會先熬夜溫習測驗，之後又花一小時看影片或者和朋友聊天。如果他經常這樣做，那麼早起就會變成一場硬仗，尤其是在那些不用急著出門的日子裡更是如此。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a secondary school student might stay up revising
for a test, then spend another hour watching videos or chatting with friends. If
they do that regularly, waking up early becomes an uphill battle, especially on
days when they do not have to rush out of the house.
[例如，一個中學生可能會先熬夜溫習測驗，之後又花一小時看影片或者和朋友聊天。如果
他經常這樣做，那麼早起就會變成一場硬仗，尤其是在那些不用急著出門的日子裡更是如

components.english: Overall, I’d say children and youngsters tend to wake up late because of a mix of biology and lifestyle. Their bodies ask for more sleep, and modern routines often push bedtime later, so the result is almost written in the stars.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說小孩子和年輕人之所以常常較遲起床，是因為生理因素和生活方式交織在一起。他們的身體本來就需要較多睡眠，而現代生活習慣又往往把入睡時間推得更後，所以出現這樣的結果，其實幾乎是很自然的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say children and youngsters tend to wake up late
because of a mix of biology and lifestyle. Their bodies ask for more sleep, and
modern routines often push bedtime later, so the result is almost written in the
stars.
[總結來說，我會說小孩子和年輕人之所以常常較遲起床，是因為生理因素和生活方式交織
在一起。他們的身體本來就需要較多睡眠，而現代生活習慣又往往把入睡時間推得更後，所
以出現這樣的結果，其實幾乎是很自然的。]

question.english: Do you know anyone who likes to get up early?
question.chinese: 你認識有人喜歡早起嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you know anyone who likes to get up early?
你認識有人喜歡早起嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. My father is the first person who comes to mind, because he is very much an early bird and has always liked starting the day before everyone else.
components.chinese: 是的，當然有。我第一個想到的人就是我爸爸，因為他真的是個很早起的人，而且一向都喜歡在其他人還沒開始一天之前就先起床。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. My father is the first person who comes to mind, because
he is very much an early bird and has always liked starting the day before
everyone else.
[是的，當然有。我第一個想到的人就是我爸爸，因為他真的是個很早起的人，而且一向都
喜歡在其他人還沒開始一天之前就先起床。]

components.english: I think he enjoys early mornings because they give him a sense of calm and control. Before the phones start ringing, the traffic builds up, and the day begins pulling people in ten different directions, everything feels quieter and clearer. For him, that time is like a small pocket of peace. He can think straight, ease into the day, and get things done without feeling rushed.
components.chinese: 我覺得他喜歡早晨，是因為那會帶給他一種平靜和掌控感。在電話開始響、交通開始繁忙，以及一天把人往不同方向拉扯之前，一切都顯得更安靜、更清晰。對他來說，那段時間就像一個小小的平靜空間。他可以更清楚地思考，更從容地進入一天，也可以在不趕不忙的情況下把事情做好。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I think he enjoys early mornings because they give him a sense
of calm and control. Before the phones start ringing, the traffic builds up, and
the day begins pulling people in ten different directions, everything feels quieter
and clearer. For him, that time is like a small pocket of peace. He can think
straight, ease into the day, and get things done without feeling rushed.
[我覺得他喜歡早晨，是因為那會帶給他一種平靜和掌控感。在電話開始響、交通開始繁忙
，以及一天把人往不同方向拉扯之前，一切都顯得更安靜、更清晰。對他來說，那段時間就
像一個小小的平靜空間。他可以更清楚地思考，更從容地進入一天，也可以在不趕不忙的情
況下把事情做好。]

components.english: For example, even on weekends, he often gets up around six, makes himself a cup of tea, goes for a walk, and reads the news. By the time the rest of the family is just opening their eyes, he has already got the ball rolling and feels fully awake.
components.chinese: 例如，即使到了週末，他也常常六點左右就起床，替自己泡一杯茶、出去散步，然後看看新聞。等到家裡其他人剛剛睜開眼時，他通常已經開始了自己的一天，而且整個人都很清醒。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, even on weekends, he often gets up around six, makes
himself a cup of tea, goes for a walk, and reads the news. By the time the rest of
the family is just opening their eyes, he has already got the ball rolling and feels
fully awake.
[例如，即使到了週末，他也常常六點左右就起床，替自己泡一杯茶、出去散步，然後看看新
聞。等到家裡其他人剛剛睜開眼時，他通常已經開始了自己的一天，而且整個人都很清

components.english: So yes, I do know someone who likes getting up early, and for him it is not a burden at all; it is simply the rhythm that suits him best.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認識有人喜歡早起，而對他來說，那完全不是一種負擔；那只是最適合他的生活節奏。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do know someone who likes getting up early, and for
him it is not a burden at all; it is simply the rhythm that suits him best.
[所以，是的，我的確認識有人喜歡早起，而對他來說，那完全不是一種負擔；那只是最適合
他的生活節奏。]

components.english: On top of that, I think he likes getting up early because it puts him in a better mood and helps him stay disciplined.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我覺得他喜歡早起，也是因為那會讓他心情更好，並且幫助他保持自律。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think he likes getting up early because it puts him in
a better mood and helps him stay disciplined.
[除此之外，我覺得他喜歡早起，也是因為那會讓他心情更好，並且幫助他保持自律。]

components.english: Some people come alive at night, whereas others seem to bloom in the morning, and he is definitely the second type. If he gets up early, he tends to feel fresh, productive, and ready to take on the day. If he sleeps in too late, he actually feels a bit sluggish, as though the day has slipped through his fingers before it has even properly begun. So, for him, waking up early is not just a habit; it sets the tone for everything that follows.
components.chinese: 有些人到了晚上才真正有精神，而另一些人則像是在清晨才真正綻放，而他明顯就是後者。如果他早起，他通常會覺得自己精神更好、更有效率，也更準備好面對一天。相反，如果他睡得太晚，他反而會覺得有點懶散，好像一天還沒真正開始，就已經從指縫間溜走了。所以對他來說，早起不只是一個習慣；它其實替接下來的一切定下了基調。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some people come alive at night, whereas others seem to
bloom in the morning, and he is definitely the second type. If he gets up early,
he tends to feel fresh, productive, and ready to take on the day. If he sleeps in
too late, he actually feels a bit sluggish, as though the day has slipped through
his fingers before it has even properly begun. So, for him, waking up early is not
just a habit; it sets the tone for everything that follows.
[有些人到了晚上才真正有精神，而另一些人則像是在清晨才真正綻放，而他明顯就是後
者。如果他早起，他通常會覺得自己精神更好、更有效率，也更準備好面對一天。相反，如果
他睡得太晚，他反而會覺得有點懶散，好像一天還沒真正開始，就已經從指縫間溜走了。所
以對他來說，早起不只是一個習慣；它其實替接下來的一切定下了基調。]

components.english: For instance, on days when he wakes up early, he is usually more cheerful and more energetic at breakfast. He chats more, moves faster, and seems more switched on. That contrast is quite easy to notice.
components.chinese: 例如，在他早起的日子裡，他通常在早餐時會比較開朗，也比較有精神。他會聊得更多、動作更俐落，而且整個人看起來更進入狀態。這種對比其實很容易看得出來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, on days when he wakes up early, he is usually more
cheerful and more energetic at breakfast. He chats more, moves faster, and seems
more switched on. That contrast is quite easy to notice.
[例如，在他早起的日子裡，他通常在早餐時會比較開朗，也比較有精神。他會聊得更多、動
作更俐落，而且整個人看起來更進入狀態。這種對比其實很容易看得出來。]

components.english: Overall, yes, I do know someone who likes to get up early. To him, it is not about forcing himself out of bed; it is about buying a little peace, clarity, and momentum before the rest of the world comes knocking.
components.chinese: 總結來說，是的，我的確認識有人喜歡早起。對他來說，那並不是勉強自己離開床鋪，而是在世界開始敲門之前，先為自己換來一點平靜、一點清晰，以及一天向前推進的力量。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, yes, I do know someone who likes to get up early. To
him, it is not about forcing himself out of bed; it is about buying a little peace,
clarity, and momentum before the rest of the world comes knocking.
[總結來說，是的，我的確認識有人喜歡早起。對他來說，那並不是勉強自己離開床鋪，而是
在世界開始敲門之前，先為自己換來一點平靜、一點清晰，以及一天向前推進的力量。]
